services.name: Enterprise → Premier/Pro Standard
services.tagline: Drop your clean Enterprise file, walk away with a Premier or Pro copy.
services.description: Convert your QuickBooks Enterprise file to Premier or Pro. Standard complexity files with clean data, processed in under 60 minutes. Includes list verification and data integrity check.
services.meta_description: Convert clean QuickBooks Enterprise files to Premier/Pro in under 60 minutes. Includes list verification and data integrity check. Canadian.
services.deliverable: Enterprise → Premier/Pro conversion of your .qbm file
longDescription: Premier and Pro lock you out of any company file that was last opened in QuickBooks Enterprise, which is the reason most Canadian accounting practices keep paying the Enterprise per-seat tag for years longer than they actually need to. The Standard service inside this NexFortis QuickBooks Portal handles that exact downgrade in a single round trip. You upload a clean .qbm portable file, our team rebuilds the data in Premier or Pro Desktop on a current release, runs a list-by-list verification, and ships back a working .qbm that you restore on your own workstation.

We file a job under Standard when the source matches the unmessy half of the Enterprise install base: a single company, a tidy chart of accounts, no advanced inventory turned on, and list totals that fit comfortably inside the Premier ceiling on customers, vendors, items, and employees combined. If your file is missing any of those characteristics our intake step routes you to the Complex tier instead, but in our queue the majority of incoming Enterprise files qualify for Standard, and that is what keeps the per-job clock so short.

Every Standard conversion rebuilds account balances penny-perfect against the Enterprise source. We compare opening balances, closing trial balances, A/R and A/P agings, undeposited funds, sales tax payable, and payroll liability accounts on both sides before the file leaves our environment. The verification stage also confirms list counts haven't drifted, customer balances reconcile per name, and bill payments still tie back to their originating bills. If any of those checks fail we fix the gap before delivery — we never bill for a job we cannot stand behind.

Pricing on this NexFortis QuickBooks Portal is quoted in Canadian dollars, GST or HST is added at checkout based on your billing province, and every order is backed by our written money-back guarantee. The launch rate of $75.00 CAD covers the conversion itself, the verification report, and two follow-up support tickets inside the next fourteen days. Files are encrypted in transit, deleted from our processing host after seven days, and are never used to train any external model. The deliverable is your own company data rebuilt in Premier or Pro.

Most clients restore the converted file the same business day we send it back. We target currently-supported Premier and Pro releases on Windows 10 and Windows 11, so the .qbm is ready for either Desktop family without a separate compatibility step. Your usernames, password policies, customer types, vendor types, classes, payroll history, and transaction detail all carry over unchanged. The only thing that actually changes is the file format — your bookkeeper opens the new copy in Premier or Pro and continues posting where Enterprise left off, with no relearning curve at all.

featureSections.heading: What we verify before delivery
featureSections.body: Every Standard conversion is checked against a fixed list before the .qbm leaves our processing host: the trial balance reconciles to the dollar against the Enterprise source, list counts on customers, vendors, items, and accounts match exactly, A/R and A/P aging buckets line up by name, sales tax liability rolls forward correctly, and the bank-reconciliation status on every reconciled account is preserved. We then document the result in a short verification PDF that you can hand directly to a reviewing accountant — our deliverable is a working Premier or Pro file plus the evidence that it is working.

featureSections.heading: Files we accept and what you receive back
featureSections.body: We accept QuickBooks Enterprise portable backups (.qbm) only. Use File → Create Copy → Portable company file inside Enterprise to produce one before uploading. We do not need .qbb full backups, and we never ask for the live .qbw working file because portable copies preserve the underlying data with much smaller file sizes and shorter transfer times. You receive a single converted .qbm plus the verification PDF. Restoring on your end is the same File → Open or Restore Company → Restore a portable file workflow you already use on any other QuickBooks backup.

faqs.question: Will the converted file open on every supported Premier or Pro release?
faqs.answer: Yes — the .qbm we deliver opens cleanly on currently-supported QuickBooks Desktop Premier and Pro releases running on Windows. If your firm is still on a specific legacy build we can target it on request before we start the conversion. Once the file is restored you can keep updating Premier or Pro on its own release cycle without ever opening Enterprise again, which is the entire point of moving down — predictable per-seat pricing without losing your underlying transaction history.

faqs.question: What happens if my Enterprise file does not qualify as Standard?
faqs.answer: If we open the upload and find advanced inventory turned on, multi-currency enabled, non-default custom fields, or list counts above the Premier ceiling, we pause the job and email you before processing further. You receive a clear explanation of which trigger fired and an optional upgrade quote to the Complex service. You can also cancel at that point for a full refund — we never auto-bill the larger amount, and you never end up with a half-finished file you cannot actually open.

faqs.question: How do I send my .qbm to your team securely?
faqs.answer: Every order in this Portal includes an encrypted upload link tied to your account, with 256-bit transport encryption and a per-order access token that expires after delivery. We do not accept email attachments because email is not an appropriate transport for multi-megabyte company files containing payroll history and banking details. After we deliver the converted file we retain your upload for seven days to support follow-up tickets, then we permanently delete it from our processing host.

faqs.question: Does the conversion preserve my custom reports and memorized transactions?
faqs.answer: Premier and Pro share the QuickBooks Desktop report engine with Enterprise, so memorized reports, memorized transactions, customer types, vendor types, terms, payment methods, and most other internal lookups carry across without change. The only routine casualties are Enterprise-only features such as advanced reporting and roles-based permissions that have no Premier counterpart at all — our verification report calls those out by name so you know exactly what was dropped before you start working day-to-day in the new file.

faqs.question: Are CAD prices final, or does GST/HST get added at checkout?
faqs.answer: Every price you see on this Portal is in Canadian dollars before tax. Canadian customers pay GST or HST at checkout based on the billing province, exactly the way QuickBooks itself layers sales tax on a domestic invoice. We never bill in USD, we never surprise-add a foreign-currency surcharge, and we do not charge any kind of monthly maintenance fee on top of the one-time conversion price. The line items on your invoice match the prices shown on the order page.

services.name: Enterprise → Premier/Pro Complex
services.tagline: Heavy Enterprise files with custom fields, multi-currency, or huge lists handled end-to-end.
services.description: Convert complex QuickBooks Enterprise files with advanced inventory, multi-currency, large list counts, or custom fields. Includes full data audit and integrity verification.
services.meta_description: Complex Enterprise-to-Premier/Pro conversion: advanced inventory, multi-currency, large lists, custom fields. Full data audit included.
services.deliverable: Enterprise → Premier/Pro conversion with full data audit on complex files
longDescription: Complex Enterprise files have a way of compounding small inconveniences into a project nobody at the practice wants to take on. Advanced inventory, classes layered on top of locations, custom fields nobody documented, multi-currency that someone enabled three fiscal years ago and forgot, list counts brushing the Premier ceiling — each one on its own is solvable, but stacked together they turn a one-hour conversion into a week of triage. The Complex service in this NexFortis QuickBooks Portal exists for exactly that profile, and it is the same engineers, same audit trail, same money-back guarantee.

Where the Standard service simply rebuilds your data in Premier or Pro, Complex begins with a structured pre-flight: a full diagnostic over the source file to enumerate every advanced feature in use, identify list-count headroom against the Premier name caps, flag custom-field usage by transaction type, and quantify the audit-trail and inventory-history footprint. The diagnostic output is what we use to plan the conversion path — what to migrate as-is, what to flatten, and what to surface to you as a deliberate choice before processing.

Multi-currency in particular gets careful handling on a Complex job. Intuit treats multi-currency as a one-way switch inside Enterprise, so any legacy currency-tagged transactions need to be reconciled to a single home currency on the Premier or Pro side without breaking historical reports. Our process preserves the original transaction amounts, applies your chosen translation rule per source currency, and writes a parallel audit log of every adjustment so your reviewing accountant can see exactly which lines were touched and why, down to the journal entry.

Pricing for Complex is $100.00 CAD at the launch rate (regular $199.00 CAD) and the turnaround target stays under sixty minutes for the conversion itself, with the diagnostic running in parallel rather than adding to the clock. Files move over an encrypted upload tied to your Portal account, are processed inside our isolated Canadian environment, and are deleted seven days after delivery. The deliverable is a working Premier or Pro .qbm plus a written audit document that maps every non-trivial transformation back to the source.

After delivery you keep the entire Standard checklist — penny-perfect balances, list parity, A/R and A/P agings, sales-tax rollforward — plus the Complex-only audit document and a thirty-minute review call with the engineer who ran your conversion. The review call is the part most firms tell us they value most: a single conversation with the person who actually touched the data, not a support queue, walking through each non-trivial decision before you put the file in front of a reviewing partner or close out the engagement.

featureSections.heading: When to choose Complex over Standard
featureSections.body: Pick the Complex tier when any of the following apply to your source file: advanced inventory is enabled (FIFO, lot/serial tracking, or bin locations), multi-currency has ever been turned on, custom fields are in use beyond the Enterprise default set, list counts on customers, vendors, items, or employees are within ten percent of the Premier 14,500-name ceiling, classes and locations are both in use simultaneously, or you have unit-of-measure conversions defined on inventory items. If the file matches none of those, the Standard service is the right fit and saves you the upcharge.

featureSections.heading: How we handle large lists and advanced inventory
featureSections.body: Premier and Pro do not support advanced inventory, so any FIFO, lot, serial, or bin data has to be flattened into a structure both editions can carry forward without breaking historical reports. Our pipeline preserves quantity-on-hand and average cost per item exactly, archives the advanced-inventory metadata to a structured CSV that you can re-import if you ever scale back into Enterprise, and writes adjustment transactions on the converted file rather than back-dating quantity changes. The result is a Premier or Pro file that ties to the source and a clean off-ramp if requirements change later.

faqs.question: How is Complex different from the Standard service in practice?
faqs.answer: Standard runs as a fixed pipeline on a clean source file and ships in a single round trip. Complex begins with a separate diagnostic pass on your data, may pause for confirmation if the diagnostic flags an irreversible decision (collapsing currencies, dropping advanced inventory metadata, retiring deprecated custom fields), and finishes with a written audit document plus a review call. Same engineers, same delivery format, same .qbm output — but the work needed to land that output safely is materially larger, which is why the rate sits a tier above Standard.

faqs.question: What happens to advanced inventory data after conversion?
faqs.answer: Premier and Pro do not have a place to store FIFO costing layers, lot or serial tracking, or bin locations, so we flatten that data into the historical record and archive the original metadata into a structured CSV that ships alongside the .qbm. Quantity-on-hand and average cost stay accurate on every item, ledger balances tie back to the Enterprise source to the cent, and the archive lets you reconstruct the advanced-inventory state if you ever migrate back up the SKU stack later in the company's life.

faqs.question: Can you keep my custom fields on customers, vendors, and items?
faqs.answer: Premier and Pro support a smaller set of custom fields than Enterprise, so we map your existing custom fields against the destination edition's capacity and report any overflow before the conversion starts. Where the destination has room, the field name and per-record values come across unchanged. Where the destination cannot carry a field, we archive it to the same audit document we use for currency and inventory decisions, with the per-record values exported to CSV so a future tool can re-import them on demand.

faqs.question: Will the thirty-minute review call cover all my reviewers' questions?
faqs.answer: The review call covers every decision the diagnostic flagged on your specific file, the verification numbers from our standard checklist, and any follow-up points your reviewing accountant raises in the days after delivery. Most firms only need a single call. If your reviewer surfaces additional questions inside the fourteen-day support window we answer them through the Portal at no extra charge — we do not run a per-minute meter on conversions, and we do not punt complex follow-ups into a separate paid engagement.

faqs.question: Is the Complex service still under sixty minutes end to end?
faqs.answer: The conversion itself targets the same under-sixty-minute clock as Standard. The diagnostic that gates the conversion runs in parallel rather than in series, so it does not extend the wall-clock time you see in the Portal. The thirty-minute review call is scheduled separately at your convenience, usually within forty-eight hours of delivery, and is not on the conversion clock. If a diagnostic-flagged decision needs your input we pause and notify you, which can extend the total elapsed time but never the active processing minutes you are billed for.

services.name: Guaranteed 30-Minute Conversion
services.tagline: A guaranteed thirty-minute turnaround on Enterprise conversions when a deadline cannot wait.
services.description: Priority processing with a guaranteed 30-minute turnaround. Same thorough conversion with expedited delivery. Perfect for urgent deadlines.
services.deliverable: Enterprise → Premier/Pro conversion delivered within 30 minutes, guaranteed
longDescription: Some Enterprise conversions cannot share a queue with anything else. A reviewing partner is on a plane in two hours, a CRA submission window closes at the end of the business day, a buyer's accountant is sitting in a meeting room waiting for the file — these are the calls we hear weekly, and they are why this NexFortis QuickBooks Portal carries a Guaranteed 30-Minute Conversion as a distinct, separately priced service. Order it and your file is pulled to the front of every other ticket in our system, with a written turnaround promise behind it.

The thirty-minute clock in this service starts the moment your encrypted upload finishes inside the Portal, not when the order is placed. We size the engineering rotation so a senior conversion engineer is always available during posted business hours; if your file lands outside business hours, the clock starts at the next opening bell and you keep the priority slot for that morning. We publish business hours plainly on the order page and confirm them in the order receipt before you commit, so the guarantee never becomes a surprise.

Every step of the Standard conversion still runs — penny-perfect balance verification, list-count parity, A/R and A/P agings, sales-tax liability rollforward, bank-reconciliation status preservation — they simply run on a dedicated processing host with no queue ahead of them. The thirty-minute promise is about scheduling, not about cutting checks. We never ship a file that fails our verification just to hit the clock; if a real issue surfaces during processing we contact you immediately and you choose between a refund and a slower correct delivery.

Pricing is $125.00 CAD at the launch rate (regular $249.00 CAD), GST or HST added at checkout, and the deliverable is the same .qbm plus verification PDF you would receive on a regular Standard order. The premium covers the dedicated engineer, the priority queue access, and the written guarantee — if we miss the thirty-minute target for a reason that is on us, your conversion is fully refunded and you keep the converted file at no charge. We do not stretch the definition of 'on us' to wriggle out of the refund.

Use this service when a missed deadline costs more than the price difference. Most clients reach for it during quarter-end accountant reviews, business-sale due diligence sprints, CRA audit-response windows, and short-fuse migrations where the previous bookkeeper held the file too long. If your timing is comfortable, the Standard service ships in well under sixty minutes anyway and saves you the premium — we will tell you that during pre-purchase chat if you ask. We would rather you buy the right tier than the most expensive tier.

featureSections.heading: How the thirty-minute guarantee works
featureSections.body: The clock begins when your encrypted upload finishes inside the Portal during posted business hours. A senior engineer pulls the job ahead of every other ticket, runs the full Standard checklist on a dedicated processing host, and ships the converted .qbm plus verification PDF before the thirty-minute mark. If we miss the target for a reason on our side — capacity, tooling, engineer availability — your conversion is refunded in full and you keep the file at no charge. The guarantee is in writing on every receipt, so there is nothing for either side to interpret after the fact.

featureSections.heading: When this service is the right call
featureSections.body: Pick the Guaranteed 30-Minute service when a missed window has a downstream cost: an accountant review meeting that is already booked, a CRA submission portal that closes at end of day, a business-sale due-diligence checklist that is gating a transaction, or a year-end soft close that has to wrap before the bank cuts off. If you have flex on timing, the Standard service ships under sixty minutes anyway and is materially less expensive. We will tell you that during pre-purchase chat if you ask, because the right product call is what keeps you in this Portal long-term.

faqs.question: What exactly counts as missing the thirty-minute target?
faqs.answer: Missing the target means the converted .qbm is not in your Portal download area within thirty minutes of upload completion during posted business hours, for any reason that is not your input — engineer availability, processing-host capacity, tooling failure, or a queue mistake on our side. If the file fails our verification mid-job we pause and contact you, the clock stops, and the guarantee converts to a full refund at your option. The trigger is purely time-on-our-side, not the absence of post-delivery support tickets.

faqs.question: Does the guarantee apply to Complex files or only Standard?
faqs.answer: The Guaranteed 30-Minute Conversion is sized for the Standard pipeline — clean source, no advanced inventory, no multi-currency, no oversized lists. If your file matches the Complex profile our diagnostic flags it on intake and we contact you before starting; you can either step down to Complex (which targets sixty minutes) or cancel for a full refund. We never run a Complex-shaped file through the thirty-minute lane silently because the work simply does not fit the slot, and we will not bill the premium for a service that cannot deliver.

faqs.question: What if I upload outside business hours — does the clock start anyway?
faqs.answer: No. The clock starts at the next opening of posted business hours, and your job keeps the priority slot for that morning so it is the first conversion processed once the team is back on shift. We confirm business hours plainly in the order receipt before you commit so this is never a surprise. If you absolutely need a conversion completed before the next business day we have an after-hours escalation path; contact pre-purchase support through this Portal and we will scope it directly.

faqs.question: Will I still receive the verification PDF on a thirty-minute order?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Guaranteed 30-Minute service runs the same verification checklist as the Standard service — trial balance reconciliation, list-count parity, A/R and A/P aging match, sales-tax rollforward, bank-reconciliation status preservation. The PDF lands in your Portal download area alongside the converted .qbm at the same time. The thirty-minute promise covers both deliverables in one envelope; we do not deliver the file inside the window and then drag the verification document out into a separate clock.

services.name: File Health Check
services.tagline: An expert read on what is bloating, breaking, or slowing your QuickBooks file today.
services.description: Comprehensive diagnostic scan of your QuickBooks file. Identifies data integrity issues, bloated lists, orphaned transactions, and optimization opportunities.
services.deliverable: Detailed diagnostic report on your QuickBooks file's health
longDescription: QuickBooks files do not announce that they have crossed the line from healthy to unhealthy — performance just slowly degrades, the audit trail balloons, lists get harder to scroll, the Verify Data utility starts taking longer to finish, and one day a routine task in Premier or Enterprise that used to take a minute is suddenly a five-minute coffee break. The File Health Check service in this NexFortis QuickBooks Portal is a structured diagnostic that surfaces every contributor to that drift, ranks them by how much they cost you in performance and risk, and tells you which ones are worth fixing.

We look at twelve categories on every file we open: data integrity (the same Verify pass QuickBooks runs internally, plus a few of our own checks), audit-trail size, list bloat across customers, vendors, items, and accounts, orphaned transactions, undeposited-funds residue, sales-tax-liability roll, A/R and A/P aging anomalies, bank-reconciliation drift, payroll-liability accuracy, attachment storage footprint, and total file size against the practical Premier or Enterprise ceiling. Each category gets its own page in the report so a reviewing partner can flip directly to whatever they are accountable for that morning.

The deliverable is a written health report, not a verbal summary. Every finding ships with three things: the underlying evidence (counts, file-size deltas, trial-balance impact), our recommendation in plain language, and an estimate of how much faster the file would feel if the recommendation were implemented. We also tell you when a finding is cosmetic and not worth touching — most files have a few of those, and naming them up front saves you and your bookkeeper from chasing a fix that does not move the needle in real-world use.

Pricing on the Health Check is $25.00 CAD at the launch rate (regular $49.00 CAD) and the turnaround target is same-business-day during posted hours. The diagnostic does not modify your data — we open a copy in our isolated Canadian environment, run the checks, and discard the working copy after the report is written. The .qbm you uploaded is retained for seven days in case you decide to follow the report up with one of our remediation services (Audit Trail Removal, Super Condense, List Reduction, Multi-Currency Removal), and is then permanently deleted.

The Health Check is the recommended starting point any time you are not sure which remediation service to buy. The audit-trail removal is the right answer for files where the audit trail is the dominant size driver; super condense is the right answer when transaction history is the driver; list reduction is the right answer when names lists are the driver. Without a diagnostic to point at the actual cause, it is easy to spend on the wrong fix and end up with a file that is still slow. Twenty-five dollars buys you the right answer.

featureSections.heading: What the diagnostic actually inspects
featureSections.body: Each Health Check inspects twelve categories: data integrity (Verify Data plus our own consistency checks), audit-trail size and growth rate, list bloat across customers, vendors, items, and accounts, orphaned and unmatched transactions, undeposited-funds residue, sales-tax-liability rollforward accuracy, A/R and A/P aging anomalies, bank-reconciliation drift on every reconciled account, payroll-liability accuracy against the underlying payroll items, attachment storage footprint, and total file size relative to the practical Premier or Enterprise ceiling. Every category is reported as a separate finding, ranked by impact, with concrete numbers attached so the recommendation is auditable.

featureSections.heading: How the report is structured for reviewers
featureSections.body: The report is laid out so a reviewing partner can read only the executive summary and still walk away with an informed opinion. Page one ranks every finding by performance impact and gives a one-line recommendation per finding. Pages two onward expand each finding with raw counts, file-size deltas, and the specific remediation service that addresses it (with launch price quoted in Canadian dollars). The final page lists everything we explicitly looked at and found healthy, so reviewers see the negative-space evidence too — what is not broken is documented alongside what is.

featureSections.heading: What we will not do during a Health Check
featureSections.body: The Health Check is read-only. We do not condense your file, remove your audit trail, merge any names, archive any transactions, or modify a single line of data on the source. The diagnostic runs against an isolated copy and the working copy is destroyed when the report is finalized. If a finding makes you want to act, the report names the specific remediation service in this Portal and links you straight to its order page — but the Health Check itself never touches the production file, which is why same-day turnaround is an honest promise instead of a stretch goal.

faqs.question: Do I have to act on every finding in the Health Check report?
faqs.answer: No. The report ranks findings by real-world impact precisely so you can choose where to spend remediation budget instead of treating every finding as a must-fix. Most files end up with two or three findings worth acting on and a longer tail of cosmetic items the report explicitly tells you to ignore. We are not paid by the number of follow-up services you buy, so we do not pad the recommendations — the goal is the smallest set of fixes that returns your file to healthy.

faqs.question: How does the Health Check compare to QuickBooks Verify Data?
faqs.answer: Verify Data inside QuickBooks runs an internal consistency check and either reports OK or surfaces a generic error code. The Health Check does the same Verify pass for completeness, then layers our own audit-trail, list-bloat, orphaned-transaction, sales-tax, payroll, and reconciliation checks on top, with concrete numbers attached. Verify tells you if the file is broken; the Health Check tells you why the file is slow, where the bloat sits, and which remediation moves the needle most.

faqs.question: Will running the Health Check change anything in my file?
faqs.answer: No. The diagnostic is read-only. We open a copy of your .qbm in an isolated Canadian processing environment, run every inspection against that copy, write the report, and then discard the working copy. Your original .qbm on our processing host is retained for seven days in case you decide to follow up with a remediation service and is then permanently deleted. The file that lives in QuickBooks on your machine is never opened, modified, or rewritten by our team during a Health Check.

faqs.question: Can I use the Health Check as an audit-readiness step?
faqs.answer: Yes — many practices buy the Health Check before a CRA submission, a year-end review, or a due-diligence engagement specifically to surface unflattering findings under their own control rather than discovering them mid-audit. The report is written so it can be handed to a reviewer as evidence of file integrity, with every healthy category explicitly named alongside the findings. If the diagnostic surfaces something material we recommend a specific remediation service in the Portal so the audit trail of work done is also clean.

services.name: Rush Delivery
services.tagline: Skip the wait by promoting your existing order ahead of every other ticket.
services.description: Jump to the front of the processing queue. Your order is prioritized above all standard orders.
services.deliverable: Front-of-queue priority placement on your existing order
longDescription: Rush Delivery is the lever you pull when a service you have already ordered in this NexFortis QuickBooks Portal needs to land sooner than the regular queue allows. It is not a separate conversion — it is a priority flag attached to an existing order so your file is pulled forward of every ticket that is not also flagged as rush. Buy it any time before processing starts and it applies retroactively; buy it after delivery and we will refund the charge automatically because the lever has nothing left to pull on.

The mechanic is intentionally simple. Standard orders run in a first-in, first-served queue under our posted same-day or under-sixty-minute targets, depending on the service. Rush Delivery moves your specific ticket to the head of that queue ahead of any non-rush ticket already waiting, and any non-rush ticket that arrives after yours stays behind it. It does not override the Guaranteed 30-Minute Conversion, which has its own dedicated processing lane, and it does not stack with that service either — the thirty-minute guarantee already includes the priority lane Rush Delivery would otherwise grant.

Pricing is $25.00 CAD at the launch rate (regular $49.00 CAD), GST or HST added at checkout, and the charge is independent of the underlying service price. You can attach Rush to any non-thirty-minute conversion order at the time of purchase, or add it to an existing in-queue order through the Portal as long as processing has not started. The interface confirms the new placement and shows the revised expected delivery window before you commit, so the value of the rush is always visible up front rather than implied.

Real-world use cases tend to cluster around external deadlines: an accountant who is reviewing the file the next morning, a buyer's CPA who is on a flight tomorrow, a CRA window that closes at five o'clock, a payroll cutoff that the previous bookkeeper missed. If your timing pressure is internal — an end-of-month soft close, a partner meeting later in the week — the regular queue is usually plenty fast and you save the upcharge. We will tell you that pre-purchase if you ask, because we would rather match the lever to the actual problem.

Rush Delivery has no effect on the verification work, the deliverable, or the support window that comes with the underlying service. You receive the same .qbm or report, the same verification PDF, the same fourteen-day support tickets, and the same money-back guarantee — just sooner. If we accept a Rush charge and then fail to materially advance your delivery time relative to the regular queue (because, for example, the queue happened to be empty when you ordered), the Rush portion is refunded automatically without you having to ask for it.

featureSections.heading: What Rush Delivery actually does
featureSections.body: Rush Delivery moves your existing order to the head of the standard processing queue ahead of every non-rush ticket in line, and keeps any non-rush ticket that arrives after yours behind it for the duration of the engagement. It does not change the verification work, the deliverable format, or the support window — it only changes scheduling. The Portal updates your expected delivery window the moment the rush flag is applied, so you always see the value of the upcharge before you commit to it instead of after the fact.

featureSections.heading: When Rush is the wrong purchase
featureSections.body: Skip Rush Delivery if you have already ordered the Guaranteed 30-Minute Conversion — that service includes the priority lane and a written time guarantee, so adding Rush is duplicate spending. Skip it also when the regular queue is fast enough for your timeline; same-day services in the Portal already run inside business hours, and standard conversions ship in well under sixty minutes most days. We will tell you pre-purchase if your specific order is unlikely to benefit from a rush so the lever stays meaningful when you actually need it.

featureSections.heading: Refund policy on un-needed Rush
featureSections.body: If we accept a Rush charge on an order that does not actually move to the head of a populated queue — because the queue was already empty, because the underlying service was upgraded to thirty-minute mid-flight, or because we shipped your file in the regular window anyway — the Rush portion is refunded automatically the same day delivery completes. You do not need to file a ticket or ask, and the refund posts to the same payment method that funded the original Portal order. The auto-refund logic is documented in the Portal's order-history view so the math is auditable.

faqs.question: Can Rush Delivery be added after I have already placed an order?
faqs.answer: Yes — as long as your order has not entered active processing, you can attach Rush Delivery to it through the Portal's order detail page. The interface confirms the revised expected delivery window before you commit, so the value of the rush is visible up front. If processing has already begun, the lever has nothing left to pull on and the Portal blocks the upgrade automatically rather than charging you for an upgrade that cannot deliver any actual time savings on your specific ticket.

faqs.question: Does Rush Delivery stack with the Guaranteed 30-Minute Conversion?
faqs.answer: No. The Guaranteed 30-Minute Conversion already includes the priority lane that Rush Delivery would otherwise add, plus a written time guarantee, so stacking them is duplicate spending. The Portal will not let you add Rush to a thirty-minute order because it would do nothing the underlying service has not already promised. If you ever see Rush eligible on a thirty-minute order, treat that as a bug and let pre-purchase support know — the rule is enforced both in the cart and in the order-detail upgrade flow.

faqs.question: What if Rush turns out not to actually advance my delivery?
faqs.answer: The Portal automatically refunds the Rush portion of the order if your file ships in the same window it would have shipped without the upgrade — for example, when the queue was already empty, or when we matched your delivery to the regular target rather than beating it. You do not have to file a ticket or chase the refund. The credit posts to the same payment method that funded the original order on the same business day delivery completes, and the order-history view shows the refund line item.

faqs.question: Does Rush Delivery affect the verification or support window?
faqs.answer: No. The verification work, the deliverable format, the fourteen-day support window, and the money-back guarantee on the underlying service are unchanged when Rush is attached. The only difference is scheduling — the file lands sooner. We do not abbreviate verification to hit a rush schedule, and we do not shorten the support window to compensate for a faster delivery; both ends of the engagement stay the same shape, only the start-to-delivery clock is compressed.

services.name: Extended Support
services.tagline: Five priority tickets and broader scope for thirty days after we deliver.
services.description: Upgrade your included 2-ticket post-order support to 5 tickets with 1-hour priority SLA and broader scope. Covers questions beyond your specific order for 30 days after delivery.
services.meta_description: Upgrade post-order support: 5 tickets with 1-hour priority SLA and broader scope, for 30 days after delivery of your conversion.
services.deliverable: 5 support tickets with 1-hour SLA for 30 days after delivery
longDescription: Every conversion order in this NexFortis QuickBooks Portal includes two follow-up support tickets within fourteen days of delivery, scoped to the specific order. That is enough for the vast majority of files — most clients use one ticket for a quick question and never raise a second one. Extended Support is the upgrade for the minority of engagements where two tickets and fourteen days are clearly not enough: a complex Premier rollout across multiple users, a transition that overlaps an external accountant review, a file that lands at quarter-end, anything where the questions keep coming for thirty days, not two weeks.

The upgrade does three concrete things. It lifts your ticket allotment from two to five over the support window, it lifts the response SLA from our normal one-to-two-hour business-hour target to a guaranteed one-hour priority SLA, and it broadens the scope from 'questions about your specific order' to 'questions about your QuickBooks Desktop file in general' for the duration of the window. That last point is the one most firms tell us they value: the same engineer who ran your conversion is on the line for unrelated Premier or Pro questions for thirty days, not just questions about the conversion itself.

Pricing is $25.00 CAD at the launch rate (regular $49.00 CAD) and the support window is thirty days from the date of delivery on the underlying conversion or remediation order. Tickets are filed through the Portal so there is a single audit trail and a single timestamp on response time, which keeps the SLA promise honest. Unused tickets do not roll over after the window — if you only use one ticket in thirty days, the remaining four expire — but unused tickets also do not result in a partial refund, because the value of the service is the priority SLA and the broader scope, not the ticket count alone.

The five-ticket count is sized against real usage data from the practices we work with regularly. Two tickets is the median spend; five tickets covers the ninety-fifth percentile. If your ticket usage routinely runs above that we typically suggest a different posture entirely — usually retainer-style engineering rather than per-conversion support — and the pre-purchase chat in the Portal will surface that recommendation if your account history shows it. Extended Support is for clients whose support need is meaningfully bigger than the included two tickets but still bounded by a single conversion engagement.

Extended Support is the only add-on you can attach to an order after delivery, as long as the underlying conversion's fourteen-day window has not already closed. That makes it useful as an after-the-fact upgrade when a client realizes mid-week that their question list is going to outgrow the included support — the Portal lets you upgrade in place and the SLA promise applies to every ticket from the upgrade timestamp forward. It does not retroactively re-time tickets already filed under the included two-ticket allotment, but those tickets continue to count toward your five.

featureSections.heading: What changes versus included support
featureSections.body: Included support gives you two tickets within fourteen days, scoped to questions about the specific conversion or remediation order, with a one-to-two-hour business-hour response target. Extended Support raises the count to five tickets, lengthens the window to thirty days from delivery, broadens the scope to questions about your QuickBooks Desktop file in general (not just the order itself), and tightens the response SLA to a written one-hour priority target during posted business hours. Same engineers, same Portal ticket interface, same single audit trail — only the dials are different.

featureSections.heading: How the one-hour SLA is measured
featureSections.body: The SLA clock starts when your ticket is filed in the Portal during posted business hours. We respond within one hour with either an answer to the question or a clear next step (a follow-up question, a request for a screenshot, a scheduled callback). Tickets filed outside business hours start the clock at the next opening and keep their priority placement for that morning. If we miss the SLA on a ticket for a reason on our side, that ticket is credited back to your remaining allotment automatically — you do not have to file a separate ticket to claim the credit, because the SLA is auditable from timestamps.

featureSections.heading: When Extended Support is the wrong fit
featureSections.body: Skip Extended Support if your ticket usage on past orders has been zero or one, because the included two-ticket allotment already covers that pattern with margin. Skip it also if your support need is structurally larger than five tickets a month — that is not an Extended Support shape, it is a retainer engineering shape, and the pre-purchase chat in this Portal will recommend the right posture if your history shows it. The upgrade is sized for the middle of the curve, not the long tail; honest sizing is what keeps the SLA promise sustainable.

faqs.question: Can I add Extended Support after my conversion has been delivered?
faqs.answer: Yes — Extended Support is the only add-on you can attach after delivery, as long as the underlying conversion's fourteen-day support window has not already closed. The Portal upgrade flow keeps the same engineer on the case, applies the priority SLA from the upgrade timestamp forward, and counts any tickets already filed under your included two-ticket allotment toward the new five-ticket total. Tickets filed before the upgrade do not retroactively get the priority SLA, but they continue to live in the same ticket interface.

faqs.question: Do unused tickets roll over after the thirty-day window?
faqs.answer: No. Tickets are tied to the specific support window on the underlying order — they do not roll over to a future engagement, and they do not convert into a credit. The value of Extended Support is the priority SLA and the broader scope, not the ticket count alone, which is why we size the count against real usage data instead of selling tickets as a commodity. If you find yourself routinely under-using the upgrade, the right answer is the included support, not a discounted Extended Support tier.

faqs.question: What does broader scope mean in practice during the support window?
faqs.answer: Included support is scoped to questions about the specific order — for example, why a particular customer balance reconciles a certain way after the conversion. Extended Support broadens that scope to questions about your QuickBooks Desktop file in general for the duration of the window: how to set up a memorized transaction, how to reconfigure sales tax, how to investigate an item-list bloat issue, anything that does not require us to actually edit your file. Hands-on file edits are still ordered as their own services in the Portal.

faqs.question: Is Extended Support a substitute for a retainer engagement?
faqs.answer: No, and we will say so out loud during pre-purchase chat if your account history suggests retainer usage. Extended Support is sized for engagements where the support need is meaningfully bigger than two tickets but still bounded by a single conversion or remediation order. If your firm runs five-plus QuickBooks Desktop tickets a month on an ongoing basis, that is a retainer shape, and we will scope it directly rather than letting you stack Extended Support on every order. Honest sizing is what keeps the SLA promise sustainable for everyone.

services.name: Audit Trail Removal
services.tagline: Strip years of audit-trail bloat to shrink and speed up your company file.
services.description: Remove the audit trail from your QuickBooks file to reduce file size and improve performance. Essential for files that have grown large over years of use.
services.deliverable: Complete audit trail removal from your QuickBooks company file
longDescription: QuickBooks Desktop never deletes an audit-trail row. Every edit, void, delete, re-date, and reclassification a user has made in the file's lifetime is still sitting in the audit table, and on a file that has been in active use for ten or fifteen years, the audit trail can easily dominate file size — sometimes by a multiple over the actual transaction data underneath. The Audit Trail Removal service in the NexFortis catalog opens that table, drops it, and rebuilds the indexes so the file reopens fast, runs the Verify utility quickly, and stops dragging on every search the staff runs day-to-day.

The deliverable is a same-day rebuild of your file with the audit trail removed and every other data structure preserved exactly. We do not condense any transactions, we do not merge or delete any list entries, we do not touch your reconciliation history, and we do not modify a single posted dollar. The only thing the file loses is the long tail of administrative edit history that QuickBooks accumulated invisibly behind the scenes. Most clients see a fifty-to-eighty percent reduction in file size on first delivery, with proportionate gains on backup time, Verify time, and routine search responsiveness.

Removing the audit trail does not affect transaction-level audit information that lives on the transactions themselves — bill payments still show their originating bills, journal entries still carry their full history, customer balances still tie to invoices and payments, and bank reconciliations still show the cleared status on every line. What disappears is the meta-history: the record that user A edited line three of invoice 12345 at 14:32 on June 8, 2017. If your practice retains that history outside QuickBooks (most do, in audit-software exports or PDF year-end backups), there is no operational loss.

Pricing is $50.00 CAD at the launch rate (regular $99.00 CAD) and the turnaround target is same-business-day during posted hours. The service runs on a copy of your file inside our isolated Canadian processing environment; the deliverable is a working .qbm restored on your machine through the standard File → Open or Restore Company → Restore a portable file flow. You receive a verification PDF documenting before-and-after file size, before-and-after Verify time, and a confirmation that no transaction-level data has been altered. Files are deleted from our processing host seven days after delivery.

We recommend pairing this service with the CRA Period Copy add-on for any practice that ever has to respond to a CRA audit window, because the period copy bakes a frozen, date-range slice of the pre-removal file as evidence — see the Audit Trail + CRA Period Copy Bundle for the discounted combo. If your file's bloat is driven mostly by transaction history rather than audit trail, the right service is Super Condense; if the driver is bloated names lists, the right service is List Reduction. The File Health Check service is the cheapest way to find out which driver is dominating yours.

featureSections.heading: What stays and what goes during removal
featureSections.body: Audit Trail Removal drops the meta-history table — the record of who edited which line of which transaction at which timestamp — and rebuilds the file's indexes so reopening is fast. Everything else stays untouched: every posted transaction, every customer and vendor balance, every bill payment linkage, every reconciliation status, every memorized transaction, every payroll item, every custom field. We do not remove a single name from any list, we do not collapse any historical period into a journal entry, and we do not adjust any account balance. The deliverable is the same file at the same balance, just smaller and faster.

featureSections.heading: Expected size and speed gains
featureSections.body: On files that have been in active use for ten years or more, the audit-trail table routinely accounts for fifty to eighty percent of file size, so removal yields a proportionate reduction. Backup times shrink in line with the new size, the Verify Data utility runs proportionally faster, and routine searches across customers, vendors, items, and historical transactions feel materially snappier. Files that were brushing the practical Premier ceiling typically drop a full tier and stop triggering the soft warnings QuickBooks raises when files cross size thresholds, which is a quality-of-life upgrade for everyone using the file day-to-day.

featureSections.heading: When this is the wrong service
featureSections.body: Audit Trail Removal is the right answer when the audit-trail table is the dominant size driver in your file. It is the wrong answer when the driver is transaction history (the right service there is Super Condense), bloated names lists (List Reduction), or multi-currency overhead (Multi-Currency Removal). The File Health Check service in this Portal explicitly identifies the dominant driver and recommends the corresponding remediation, so any time you are uncertain which service to buy, the Health Check at $25.00 CAD is the cheapest way to make sure the next $50.00 CAD goes against the actual problem.

faqs.question: Will my reviewing accountant see the audit trail as missing?
faqs.answer: Reviewers see the audit trail as removed, not missing — the file documents the removal date and the engineer who performed it, and the verification PDF that ships with delivery records the before-and-after file size and the integrity result. If your reviewing accountant requires the historical audit trail for a specific period, the recommended approach is to pair this service with the CRA Period Copy in a single bundle, which bakes a frozen pre-removal slice as evidence. Most reviewers accept the documented removal once the integrity result is presented.

faqs.question: Does the removal affect bank reconciliations or payroll history?
faqs.answer: No. Bank-reconciliation status on every reconciled account is preserved exactly, including which lines were cleared, which were uncleared, and the historical reconciliation summary on each account. Payroll history stays intact — every paycheque, every tax deposit, every year-to-date amount on every employee — because that data lives on the transactions themselves rather than in the audit-trail meta-table. The verification PDF specifically calls out reconciliation status and payroll integrity as separate confirmation items so reviewers see them named, not implied.

faqs.question: Is the service reversible after delivery?
faqs.answer: The audit-trail removal itself is not reversible — once the meta-history table is dropped, the per-edit history is gone for good. However, we retain your original uploaded .qbm on the processing host for seven days after delivery for exactly this scenario, and you can request the original back at any time during that window if you change your mind. After seven days the upload is permanently deleted along with all working copies, so the practical reversibility window matches the file-retention policy on this Portal.

faqs.question: How much smaller will my file actually be after removal?
faqs.answer: It depends on your file's age and how heavily users have edited transactions over its lifetime. Files less than three years old and minimally edited typically lose ten to twenty percent of file size, which is rarely worth the order on its own. Files in active use for ten years or more — the typical buyer of this service — routinely lose fifty to eighty percent of file size. The File Health Check service in this Portal can predict your specific reduction in advance, which is useful for setting expectations before placing the remediation order.

services.name: Super Condense
services.tagline: Shrink huge company files while keeping every account balance and list intact.
services.description: Dramatically reduce your QuickBooks file size by condensing old transactions while preserving account balances and list data. Ideal for sluggish files.
services.deliverable: Condensed QuickBooks file with preserved balances and lists
longDescription: QuickBooks Desktop ships a built-in Condense Data utility, but it is famously conservative about what it will actually condense and aggressive about what it will silently break. Inventory items quietly get reset, payroll history trips it up, multi-currency transactions stop it cold, and on large files the wall-clock time often runs longer than rebuilding the file by hand would. Super Condense is the QuickBooks Data Services replacement for that utility — a same-day rebuild that collapses pre-cutoff transaction history into per-period journal entries while preserving every list entry and every account balance to the cent.

The mechanic is straightforward in concept and exacting in execution. You pick a cutoff date — usually the start of a recent fiscal year — and we collapse every posted transaction before that date into a single opening journal entry per ledger account, keyed back to a 'Pre-Cutoff Opening' control account so the audit trail of the collapse is itself documented inside the file. Every transaction on or after the cutoff date stays untouched and fully drillable. The trial balance on the cutoff date matches the source file dollar-for-dollar; we verify that as a non-negotiable gate before the file leaves our environment.

Lists survive the condense intact. Customers, vendors, items, accounts, classes, locations, employees, terms, payment methods, custom fields, memorized transactions, and memorized reports all carry across exactly. The condense touches transaction tables, not list tables, which is the structural difference between Super Condense and Intuit's built-in utility — Intuit's tool will sometimes drop names that have no post-cutoff activity, while ours never does because dropping a name is a List Reduction decision, not a Super Condense decision, and they are intentionally separate services in this catalog.

Pricing is $50.00 CAD at the launch rate (regular $99.00 CAD), GST or HST added at checkout, and the turnaround target is same-business-day during posted hours. The service runs on a copy of your file in our isolated Canadian processing environment; the deliverable is a working .qbm plus a verification PDF documenting before-and-after file size, before-and-after transaction count, the chosen cutoff date, and the trial-balance match at cutoff. We retain your original .qbm on the processing host for seven days post-delivery, then permanently delete it along with all working copies.

Super Condense is the right service when transaction history is the dominant size driver in your file — large practices with a decade or more of detailed posting history, multi-entity setups that have been running on a single file for too long, file sizes that are starting to approach the practical Premier or Pro ceiling. If the dominant driver is the audit trail rather than transactions, the right service is Audit Trail Removal; if the driver is bloated names lists, it is List Reduction. The File Health Check identifies the dominant driver in advance for $25.00 CAD, which is consistently the cheapest way to put the next remediation dollar against the right target.

featureSections.heading: What gets condensed and what does not
featureSections.body: Super Condense collapses every posted transaction dated before your chosen cutoff into a single per-account opening journal entry that ties back to a documented 'Pre-Cutoff Opening' control account. Transactions on or after the cutoff stay untouched and fully drillable — invoices, bills, payments, journal entries, sales receipts, payroll runs, the lot. List tables are out of scope: customers, vendors, items, accounts, classes, locations, employees, and every other list survive the condense exactly. The structural rule is that the service only touches transactions, never lists, which is why it never silently drops a name.

featureSections.heading: Verification and the trial-balance match
featureSections.body: Every Super Condense delivery is gated by a hard trial-balance match: the trial balance on the cutoff date in the condensed file equals the trial balance on the cutoff date in the source file dollar-for-dollar, on every account, and the verification PDF documents that match line by line. We also confirm A/R and A/P agings as of cutoff, sales-tax liability as of cutoff, payroll-liability accuracy as of cutoff, and bank-reconciliation status on every reconciled account. If any of those checks fail the file does not leave our environment — we fix the gap or refund the order, never a partial delivery.

featureSections.heading: Why this exists separately from the built-in Condense Data
featureSections.body: Intuit's built-in Condense Data utility is intentionally conservative about which files it will condense and known to behave oddly on multi-currency, advanced inventory, and payroll-heavy files. It also can take longer to run on a large file than rebuilding the file by hand would, with no progress visibility while it runs. Super Condense replaces that utility for the firms whose files actually hit the limits — same-day delivery, predictable wall-clock time, deterministic verification, and a clear refund posture if anything fails the integrity gate. Different shape, different target audience.

faqs.question: How do I pick the right cutoff date for the condense?
faqs.answer: Most clients pick the start of the most recent closed fiscal year, which keeps the entire current year fully drillable while collapsing the long tail of older history into the opening journals. If your business deals with regulated retention windows we recommend picking a cutoff at least one full year past the retention requirement so the open period covers any reasonable audit ask. The pre-purchase chat in the Portal will walk through your specific situation if you are not sure — this is the single decision that most affects the post-condense feel of the file.

faqs.question: Will historical reports still work for periods before the cutoff?
faqs.answer: Reports that summarize at the period level — monthly P&Ls, quarterly trial balances, annual balance sheets — continue to work correctly for periods before the cutoff because the opening journal entries roll up to the same totals. Reports that drill into individual pre-cutoff transactions no longer work because the underlying transactions have been replaced by the opening journals. If your practice needs ongoing transaction-level access to pre-cutoff data, the recommended pairing is the CRA Period Copy service, which bakes a separate frozen file at the source detail.

faqs.question: Does Super Condense work on multi-currency or payroll-heavy files?
faqs.answer: Yes — those are the file shapes Intuit's built-in Condense Data tool is most fragile against, and a major reason this service exists separately. Multi-currency transactions are condensed in their original posting currency with the per-account opening journals reflecting the home-currency balance at cutoff. Payroll runs and tax deposits are collapsed at the per-account level, with year-to-date amounts on each employee preserved as of cutoff. The verification PDF specifically lists multi-currency and payroll integrity as separate gating items so reviewers see them named.

faqs.question: How does Super Condense compare to Audit Trail Removal?
faqs.answer: They target different bloat sources. Super Condense reduces file size by collapsing transaction history before a chosen cutoff date — the right service when transaction count is the dominant driver. Audit Trail Removal reduces file size by dropping the meta-history table that records every per-edit timestamp — the right service when the audit trail itself is the dominant driver. Many large practices benefit from both, run in sequence; the File Health Check service tells you which one to run first based on actual numbers from your specific file rather than on rules of thumb.

services.name: List Reduction
services.tagline: Merge duplicates and prune dead names from your customer, vendor, and item lists.
services.description: Clean up and merge duplicate or inactive items in your QuickBooks lists (customers, vendors, items, accounts). Reduces clutter and improves searchability.
services.deliverable: Cleaned and de-duplicated customer, vendor, item, and account lists
longDescription: QuickBooks list pages get slow when the underlying tables grow past a few thousand entries — Premier and Pro start to feel sluggish on routine drop-down picks, search results return more candidates than the user can reasonably scan, and accidental name creation becomes the path of least resistance because finding the right existing name takes longer than typing a new one. List Reduction is the same-day cleanup service that brings your customers, vendors, items, and accounts back to a list size where the drop-downs are usable again, without losing a single posted transaction or a single dollar of historical activity.

The work has two halves. The first half is duplicate detection — we find the obvious cases (same legal name with different punctuation, same vendor with and without 'Inc.', same customer entered twice with different mailing addresses, same item with a typo in the SKU) and the less obvious ones (an item created in 2014 that is functionally identical to an item created in 2019, two customer records that differ only by the salesperson who entered them). The second half is the merge itself, which moves every transaction off the duplicate onto the survivor, deactivates the duplicate, and writes a structured CSV log of the entire move so the audit trail of the cleanup is reviewable.

Inactive-name pruning runs on a separate, configurable rule. By default we deactivate any name with no transaction activity in the last three years and no balance — a conservative threshold that catches the long tail without ever deactivating a name a reviewer might want to see. The rule is configurable up or down before processing starts, and any name that does not match the rule remains active and untouched. We never delete names outright because the audit-trail consequences are larger than deactivation; deactivation hides them from drop-downs without losing the historical record.

Pricing is $40.00 CAD at the launch rate (regular $79.00 CAD), GST or HST added at checkout, and the turnaround target is same-business-day during posted hours. The service runs on a copy of your file in our isolated Canadian processing environment; the deliverable is a working .qbm plus a verification PDF documenting before-and-after list counts on every list, the merge log in CSV form, and the trial-balance match — list reduction never affects account balances, but the verification still includes the balance match as a non-negotiable gate before the file leaves our environment.

List Reduction is the right service when bloated names lists are the dominant performance driver in your file — typically firms in their second decade of QuickBooks use, multi-location retailers with thousands of items, B2B operations with thousands of customers turned over once or twice. If the dominant driver is the audit trail or transaction history instead, the right services are Audit Trail Removal and Super Condense respectively. The File Health Check service explicitly identifies which driver dominates yours, which we recommend running before any of the three remediation services if there is any doubt — the diagnostic price is small enough to never regret.

featureSections.heading: What we merge and how the audit log works
featureSections.body: Each merge moves every transaction off the duplicate name onto the survivor, deactivates the duplicate, and writes a single line to a structured CSV log capturing the duplicate, the survivor, the count of transactions moved, and the timestamp of the merge. The CSV ships alongside the .qbm so the cleanup is fully auditable. We never delete a duplicate outright — deactivation hides it from drop-downs without losing the historical record, which is the right posture for reviewers who may need to see the original name months later. Survivors are picked by activity volume and recency, with ties broken in favour of the legal-format name.

featureSections.heading: How inactive-name pruning is scoped
featureSections.body: Inactive pruning runs on a configurable rule, which defaults to 'no transaction activity in the last three years and no current balance.' That threshold catches the long tail of customers and vendors who have not been touched in a decade without ever deactivating a name a reviewer might want to see. The rule is adjustable up to five years or down to two before processing starts; pre-purchase chat in the Portal walks through the right setting for your industry. Names that fail the rule are deactivated, never deleted, so the historical record stays intact and reactivation is a single click on your end.

featureSections.heading: Lists in scope and lists out of scope
featureSections.body: List Reduction covers customers, vendors, items, accounts, employees, classes, and locations — the high-volume operational lists. It does not cover terms, payment methods, customer types, vendor types, or other configuration lists, because those are typically small enough to clean by hand and the merge consequences are different. The verification PDF reports before-and-after counts on every list in scope, so the deliverable is auditable line by line. If you need cleanup on the configuration lists, that is best handled inside QuickBooks directly with our Extended Support upgrade attached for the support-ticket coverage.

faqs.question: Will any historical transactions be deleted during list reduction?
faqs.answer: No. The service is structurally incapable of deleting a transaction — every merge moves transactions from the duplicate to the survivor, and inactive-name pruning only deactivates names with no transaction activity matching the configured rule. The verification PDF gates delivery on a trial-balance match against the source file even though list reduction does not touch balances by design, specifically because we want the deliverable to document explicitly that no posting was changed. If a balance ever drifted during processing the file would not leave our environment.

faqs.question: How are duplicate-name candidates actually identified?
faqs.answer: We run a deterministic similarity rule across each list — exact match after punctuation normalization, case folding, and whitespace collapse, plus a configurable fuzzy match for transposed characters and obvious typos. Every candidate is paired with the suggested survivor and the merge is run only after the structured CSV log captures the pairing. Names that look similar but represent different real-world entities (two different 'John Smith' customers in different cities, for example) are kept separate by default; pre-processing chat reviews any ambiguous cases your file flags.

faqs.question: Can I exclude specific names from the cleanup before processing?
faqs.answer: Yes — pre-processing chat in the Portal lets you flag any names that should be excluded from both merging and inactive pruning regardless of activity history. Common exclusions include placeholder customers used for cash sales, vendor records used for one-time payments, and item records that are intentionally retained for historical reporting even though they have no recent activity. The exclusion list is recorded in the verification PDF so the deliverable documents not just what was changed but what was deliberately left alone, which reviewers appreciate.

faqs.question: Does the cleanup affect inventory quantities or reorder points?
faqs.answer: No. Item-level merges keep the survivor's quantity-on-hand, average cost, and reorder configuration, and add the duplicate's posted transactions to the survivor's history without changing any quantities. The verification PDF reports per-item quantity-on-hand against the source file as a separate gating item, so any drift would block delivery. If your reorder points need adjustment after the cleanup we recommend doing that inside QuickBooks once the new list shape is in front of you, since the right reorder level depends on the new aggregated history.

services.name: Multi-Currency Removal
services.tagline: Reverse the one-way multi-currency switch with every transaction kept intact.
services.description: Remove multi-currency from your QuickBooks file. Once enabled, Intuit provides no way to turn it off — we handle it safely with full data preservation.
services.deliverable: Multi-currency disabled with all transaction data preserved
longDescription: Intuit treats multi-currency in QuickBooks Desktop as a one-way switch: once a user clicks Enable Multi-Currency in the preferences, the option to disable it disappears from the interface and Intuit's documentation simply notes that the change is permanent. That is a reasonable engineering posture for a feature that touches every transaction table — but it is also a problem for the substantial number of files where multi-currency was turned on by accident, by a previous bookkeeper, or by a former owner who used to invoice in USD and no longer does. Multi-Currency Removal is the QuickBooks Data Services answer for those files.

The service rebuilds your company file with multi-currency disabled at the engine level while preserving every historical transaction in its original posting currency. Foreign-currency transactions stay readable in their source amount, the home-currency conversion stays at the original posted rate, and the trial balance in your home currency matches the source file dollar-for-dollar at every period close. What disappears is the active multi-currency apparatus — the foreign-currency drop-downs in transaction forms, the per-name preferred currency, the daily exchange-rate refresh, and every other piece of UI that exists only to support new foreign-currency posting going forward.

Multi-currency removal is materially more involved than the other QuickBooks Data Services entries in this Portal, which is reflected in both the price and the one-to-two-business-day turnaround. The diagnostic phase enumerates every foreign-currency transaction in your file, every multi-currency-tagged name on every list, and every preference and report definition that references multi-currency, and produces a structured plan for the rebuild. Processing then runs through the plan deterministically, with a checkpoint at the end of each major stage so any unexpected condition triggers a pause-and-confirm rather than a silent best-guess.

Pricing is $75.00 CAD at the launch rate (regular $149.00 CAD), GST or HST added at checkout. The service runs in our isolated Canadian processing environment and the deliverable is a working .qbm plus a written audit document mapping every non-trivial transformation back to the source — same shape as the audit document in the Complex Enterprise conversion service, because the level of forensic detail is comparable. We retain your original .qbm on the processing host for seven days post-delivery, then permanently delete it along with all working copies and the working audit document.

Use this service when multi-currency was enabled and the practice no longer needs the apparatus — most commonly an inadvertent enablement, a one-time foreign customer that has since gone away, or a business sale that retired the foreign-currency line. Use it also as a precondition to a downgrade to Pro, which has weaker multi-currency support than Premier, or to a clean QBO migration where multi-currency would otherwise need to be re-architected on the destination side. If you are not sure whether your file even has multi-currency enabled, the File Health Check identifies it in writing for $25.00 CAD.

featureSections.heading: What is preserved and what is removed
featureSections.body: Every historical transaction is preserved in its original posting currency at its original posted exchange rate, with the home-currency conversion intact. What is removed is the active multi-currency apparatus going forward: the foreign-currency drop-down on new transaction forms, the per-name preferred currency, the daily exchange-rate refresh, the multi-currency-only reports, and the preference flag itself. The trial balance in your home currency matches the source file dollar-for-dollar at every period close. The audit document delivered with the .qbm maps each non-trivial transformation back to the source so the deliverable is fully reviewable.

featureSections.heading: Why this requires one to two business days
featureSections.body: The other entries in QuickBooks Data Services target a single dimension of the file (audit trail, transaction history, list bloat) and run as deterministic same-day pipelines. Multi-currency touches every transaction table, every name list, every preference, every report definition, and the audit-trail meta-table simultaneously, which is why the work cannot fit a same-day clock honestly. The diagnostic phase alone takes longer than a typical Audit Trail Removal job from start to finish, and the rebuild has hard checkpoints between stages so any unexpected condition pauses for confirmation rather than guessing forward.

featureSections.heading: Pairing this service with a conversion or migration
featureSections.body: Multi-currency removal is a common precondition to a conversion to Pro (which has weaker multi-currency handling than Premier) and to a clean QBO migration (where multi-currency on QBO is structurally different from Desktop and rebuilding on the destination side is materially more painful). If you are buying both, ordering Multi-Currency Removal first and the conversion or migration second is almost always the right sequence. The pre-purchase chat in this Portal will scope the right combined order if your engagement spans both services so you avoid running the file through two separate audit cycles unnecessarily.

faqs.question: Will my historical foreign-currency invoices still be readable after removal?
faqs.answer: Yes. Every historical foreign-currency transaction stays in its original posting currency at its original posted exchange rate, with the home-currency conversion intact. The transaction form still shows the foreign amount and the historical rate. What changes is forward-looking: new transactions created after the rebuild are home-currency only, and the foreign-currency drop-down is no longer in the interface. The audit document records the boundary date so reviewers can immediately distinguish historical multi-currency activity from post-removal activity without having to dig through transaction-level metadata.

faqs.question: How does this differ from changing the home currency?
faqs.answer: Changing the home currency is a different operation entirely — it converts every historical transaction at a chosen exchange rate into a new home currency, which is a destructive operation we generally do not recommend on a production file. Multi-Currency Removal preserves the existing home currency and every historical posted amount; it only removes the multi-currency apparatus going forward. If you actually need a different home currency, that is a custom engagement scoped through pre-purchase chat in the Portal, not a Multi-Currency Removal job. The two services solve different problems.

faqs.question: Is the rebuild reversible if we change our mind later?
faqs.answer: The rebuild itself is not reversible at the file level — once multi-currency is disabled in the rebuilt file, re-enabling would put us right back in Intuit's one-way switch territory, only on the new file. However, we retain your original uploaded .qbm on the processing host for seven days after delivery, exactly so a change of heart in the first week falls within a reversibility window. After seven days the upload is permanently deleted. If you anticipate any chance you might want multi-currency back, hold off until that question is settled before placing the order.

faqs.question: Can I have the audit document handed directly to my reviewing accountant?
faqs.answer: Yes — the audit document is written specifically for that handoff. It is structured by file area (preferences, name lists, transaction tables, reports, audit trail), enumerates every non-trivial transformation with a reference to the source state and the post-removal state, and ends with the full set of verification gates the rebuild passed before delivery (trial-balance match, A/R and A/P agings match, sales-tax rollforward match, payroll integrity). Reviewers we have worked with consistently treat the audit document as sufficient evidence without requiring additional ad-hoc explanation calls.

services.name: QBO Readiness Report
services.tagline: Find out exactly what will break before you migrate Desktop to QuickBooks Online.
services.description: Comprehensive report analyzing your QuickBooks Desktop file's readiness for migration to QuickBooks Online. Identifies compatibility issues, data gaps, and recommended pre-migration steps.
services.meta_description: Analyze your QuickBooks Desktop file before moving to QuickBooks Online. Identifies compatibility issues, data gaps, and pre-migration steps.
services.deliverable: Written QBO migration readiness report with prioritized fixes
longDescription: QuickBooks Online and QuickBooks Desktop look similar from a distance and behave very differently up close.
