Intuit's own Desktop-to-QBO export tool will accept almost any source file, but it silently drops or simplifies a long list of features along the way: classes get flattened into a single dimension, recurring transactions are partially mapped, custom fields are reduced, advanced inventory disappears, payroll history transitions imperfectly, and reconciliation history typically does not move at all. The QBO Readiness Report is a same-day diagnostic that names every one of those landmines for your specific file before you commit to the migration.

We run the diagnostic against a copy of your .qbm in our isolated Canadian processing environment and produce a written report covering twelve categories: chart-of-accounts compatibility, list sizes against QBO's own ceilings (which are different from Desktop's), classes and locations versus QBO's tagging model, recurring transactions, advanced inventory, multi-currency posture, payroll history portability, attachment storage, custom fields, sales-tax configuration, bank-reconciliation history, and integration apps that bind to Desktop-only APIs. Each category gets a verdict (clean migration, simplified migration, manual rebuild required) plus a concrete remediation step.

The deliverable is the report, not a guarantee about what Intuit's tool will do. We are reading the source file and applying the publicly documented Intuit migration rules plus our own field experience from the Canadian practices we work with regularly. The report tells you which preferences and structures need to change in Desktop before migration to maximize fidelity, which need to be re-built in QBO after migration, and which are simply not worth carrying across at all. That up-front decision-making is the difference between a migration that lands smoothly and one that surfaces surprises three months in.

Pricing is $25.00 CAD at the launch rate (regular $49.00 CAD) and the turnaround target is same-business-day during posted hours. The diagnostic does not modify your file in any way — it runs read-only against a copy and the working copy is destroyed when the report is finalized. Your original .qbm is retained for seven days in case you decide to follow up with one of our pre-migration remediation services (Multi-Currency Removal, List Reduction, Audit Trail Removal, Super Condense), then is permanently deleted along with all working artifacts on the processing host.

We strongly recommend ordering the QBO Readiness Report before placing any irreversible decision around a Desktop-to-QBO migration. Twenty-five dollars buys the answer to the single most important question of any migration: 'what will my file actually look like once it lands.' If the report concludes that your file would migrate cleanly, the remediation cost goes to zero and you proceed with confidence. If it surfaces concrete issues, the report names the specific remediation services in this Portal that address them, with launch prices quoted in Canadian dollars so the total cost is visible up front.

featureSections.heading: What the readiness report covers
featureSections.body: The report covers twelve categories: chart-of-accounts compatibility against QBO conventions, list sizes against QBO's own ceilings, classes and locations versus QBO's tagging model, recurring transactions, advanced inventory, multi-currency posture, payroll history portability, attachment storage, custom fields, sales-tax configuration, bank-reconciliation history, and Desktop-only integration apps. Each category receives a verdict — clean migration, simplified migration, or manual rebuild required — plus a concrete remediation step quoted against the relevant service in this Portal. The structure is reusable, so a second readiness report after remediation produces a like-for-like comparison.

featureSections.heading: What the report does not promise
featureSections.body: We do not run Intuit's actual Desktop-to-QBO migration during the readiness diagnostic and we do not guarantee what Intuit's tool will do on any specific file. The report applies the publicly documented migration rules and our own field experience from Canadian practices to predict the outcome of the migration with high confidence; it is not a substitute for actually running the migration. The intent is to surface every meaningful landmine before you commit, so that the actual migration is uneventful — which is the opposite of running the migration first and discovering surprises three months in.

featureSections.heading: Why this is read-only and same-day
featureSections.body: The diagnostic does not modify your file in any way. We open a copy in our isolated Canadian processing environment, run the inspection passes, write the report, and discard the working copy. No tables are touched on the source, no transactions are re-keyed, no preferences are changed. That is what makes the same-business-day turnaround an honest target — we are not rebuilding anything, we are reading. If the report surfaces issues that need rebuilding, those rebuilds are separate paid services in this Portal and the report tells you which one fits each specific finding.

faqs.question: Should I run remediation services before or after the readiness report?
faqs.answer: Run the readiness report first. The report tells you which remediation services to buy and which ones not to bother with, which is consistently the cheapest way to allocate the next dollar. Buying remediation services blind, on the assumption that they are obviously needed, is how practices end up with a clean Multi-Currency Removal followed by a discovery that the actual migration blocker was advanced inventory all along. Twenty-five dollars to know which order to spend in is a small price relative to the cost of the wrong order.

faqs.question: Does the report cover Canadian sales-tax configuration specifically?
faqs.answer: Yes. Sales-tax configuration is one of the twelve report categories, and it is the category where Canadian practices most often hit avoidable QBO migration friction because the Desktop sales-tax structure does not map perfectly onto QBO's tax-rate model. The report enumerates every active sales-tax code and item in your Desktop file, predicts the post-migration mapping, and flags any code that would need to be rebuilt manually in QBO. We test the predictions against current QBO releases for Canadian users specifically, not the U.S. defaults that most external guidance assumes.

faqs.question: How does the report handle Desktop-only integration apps?
faqs.answer: The report enumerates every integration that has touched your Desktop file via the QuickBooks SDK or Web Connector and categorizes each by its QBO-side availability — same vendor with a QBO build, different vendor required, no QBO equivalent yet. For integrations with no QBO build, the report records what data the integration was writing to the file so you can either pause that workflow during migration or rebuild it on a different platform afterward. The category is one of the most overlooked migration risks and a major reason the report exists.

faqs.question: Will the report tell me whether QBO is actually the right move?
faqs.answer: The report is scoped to migration readiness on your specific file, not to the broader strategic question of whether QBO is the right platform for your business. That said, the report's findings often inform the strategic question — a file that scores well on readiness migrates cleanly and the strategic decision becomes easier; a file that scores poorly may be better served by staying on Desktop or by a more substantial pre-migration rebuild. Pre-purchase chat in the Portal can scope a strategic review separately if you want a stand-alone recommendation.

services.name: CRA Period Copy
services.tagline: An audit-ready slice of your books for any date range CRA asks about.
services.description: Create a clean copy of your QuickBooks file containing only data for a specific date range. Perfect for CRA audit requests or fiscal year isolation.
services.deliverable: Date-range-isolated copy of your QuickBooks file ready for CRA submission
longDescription: Canada Revenue Agency audit windows have a specific shape: a defined date range, a reasonable expectation of detailed transaction-level support, and a strong preference for a focused file rather than a full company database with sensitive forward-looking data inside it. CRA Period Copy is the QuickBooks Data Services entry that produces exactly that file: a frozen, auditable slice of your books containing every transaction inside the requested date range, exactly as posted, with no later edits and no out-of-period transactions, ready to hand to a CRA auditor or your reviewing accountant.

The output is not a condensed file or a summarized journal — every in-range transaction appears at full posting detail, including the original entry timestamp, the posting user, the audit-trail status as of the period close, and any attachment that was attached at the time. Out-of-range transactions are removed cleanly along with any forward-looking data the CRA scope does not require. List entries that were active during the period are preserved; list entries created entirely outside the range are removed unless they appear in the audit trail of an in-range transaction, in which case they remain visible.

The service is the most common pairing in this Portal with the Audit Trail Removal service, which is why we offer the Audit Trail + CRA Period Copy Bundle at a meaningful discount over buying the two separately. The bundle order produces both deliverables — the trimmed period copy for the CRA window and the post-removal main file for the practice's continuing day-to-day use — in a single round trip, with both files cross-verified so the audit trail of the bundling itself is documented. Most practices order the bundle rather than the individual services for that reason.

Pricing for the standalone CRA Period Copy is $50.00 CAD at the launch rate (regular $99.00 CAD), GST or HST added at checkout, and the turnaround target is same-business-day during posted hours. The service runs on a copy of your file inside our isolated Canadian processing environment; the deliverable is a working .qbm scoped to the requested date range plus a verification PDF that documents the period boundary, the trial-balance match at period start and period end, and the in-period transaction count. Files are encrypted in transit and deleted from our processing host seven days after delivery.

The verification PDF is structured for direct handoff to a CRA auditor or reviewing accountant. It enumerates the period boundary unambiguously (start date, end date, posting cutoff timestamp), records the trial-balance match against the source file at both endpoints, lists the in-period transaction counts by category, and confirms that no later edits have been applied to in-period transactions. Reviewers we have worked with consistently treat the PDF plus the .qbm as a self-contained audit response without requiring additional ad-hoc support, which is the entire point of producing a frozen period copy in the first place.

featureSections.heading: How the period boundary is constructed
featureSections.body: The boundary is defined by a start date and an end date plus an optional posting-cutoff timestamp, which is what we use to handle late-posted entries that hit the period after the fact. Every transaction with a transaction date inside the range is preserved at full detail; every transaction outside the range is removed. The trial balance at the start of the period and at the end of the period both match the source file dollar-for-dollar. The audit-trail status as of the period close is preserved on every in-period transaction so the reviewing party sees the same drill-down behaviour the source file would have shown at that moment in time.

featureSections.heading: What is preserved and what is removed
featureSections.body: In-period transactions are preserved at full posting detail, including the original entry timestamp, the posting user, the audit-trail status, and any attachment that was attached at the time. List entries active during the period are preserved; list entries created entirely outside the range are removed unless they appear in the audit trail of an in-period transaction. Out-of-period transactions, recurring schedules whose first instance falls outside the range, and any preference changes recorded outside the range are removed cleanly. The result is a focused file with no forward-looking data, which is the posture CRA auditors prefer.

featureSections.heading: Why this pairs with Audit Trail Removal
featureSections.body: Most practices that order Audit Trail Removal also need a CRA-ready slice of the pre-removal file as evidence of the historical state, because the audit-trail removal is irreversible. Producing the period copy first, before removal, captures that evidence cleanly. The Audit Trail + CRA Period Copy Bundle in this Portal handles the sequencing automatically and produces both files in a single round trip at a discount over buying them separately, with both files cross-verified so the audit trail of the bundling itself is documented in the verification PDF that ships with the bundle.

faqs.question: How does CRA Period Copy differ from Super Condense?
faqs.answer: Super Condense reduces file size by collapsing pre-cutoff transaction history into per-account opening journals, leaving the post-cutoff period at full transaction detail. The result is one ongoing file that is smaller and faster. CRA Period Copy keeps in-range transactions at full posting detail and removes everything outside the range, producing a frozen file with no forward-looking content. The two services are not interchangeable — Super Condense is a remediation for an active operating file, and Period Copy is an evidence file for a closed audit window. Many practices use both at different times.

faqs.question: Can the period copy include attachments and receipts?
faqs.answer: Yes. Every attachment that was attached to an in-period transaction at the time of posting is preserved with the transaction in the period copy. Attachments that were added to the source file outside the period are not carried across because they are out of scope by definition. The verification PDF reports the in-period attachment count alongside the in-period transaction count so the deliverable is auditable on attachments specifically — this matters for CRA audits that hinge on receipt evidence and for reviewing accountants who want to see attachment integrity confirmed in writing.

faqs.question: What date format should I use when ordering the period copy?
faqs.answer: Use ISO 8601 date format (YYYY-MM-DD) for both the start date and the end date when placing the order in the Portal. The order form will accept a few other formats but ISO is the unambiguous standard and avoids any month-day-year versus day-month-year confusion. The verification PDF echoes the period boundary back in ISO format so there is no ambiguity in the deliverable either. If your CRA request specifies a particular fiscal-year convention we will default to that convention; just note it in the order details before submitting.

faqs.question: Is the period copy the same as making a backup of the file?
faqs.answer: No. A standard QuickBooks backup contains the entire company file at a moment in time — every transaction, every list, every preference, every forward-looking item. A CRA Period Copy is a date-range-isolated derivative of the source file with everything outside the period cleanly removed and the trial balance verified at both endpoints. Reviewers we have worked with consistently prefer the period copy over a full backup for audit responses because it scopes the surface area to exactly the period under review without the noise and the privacy concerns of forward-looking data.

services.name: Audit Trail + CRA Period Copy Bundle
services.tagline: Trail removed, period copy prepared — our most popular CRA-readiness combo in one order.
services.description: Save with our most popular bundle: Audit Trail Removal plus CRA Period Copy. Clean up your file and isolate a specific reporting period in one order.
services.deliverable: Audit trail removal plus CRA-ready period copy in a single order
longDescription: Audit Trail Removal and CRA Period Copy are the two most-frequently-paired QuickBooks Data Services in this NexFortis QuickBooks Portal, and they are paired for a structural reason: every practice that orders an audit-trail removal needs a frozen, audit-ready slice of the pre-removal file as evidence of the historical state, because the removal itself is irreversible. The Audit Trail + CRA Period Copy Bundle exists to make that pairing the default rather than an after-the-fact realization, with the two services run in the correct sequence and cross-verified in a single delivery.

The bundle starts by producing the CRA Period Copy from your uploaded source file, against the start and end dates you supply at order time. That deliverable is set aside, then the Audit Trail Removal service runs against the same source file, producing a smaller, faster main company file for your practice's continuing day-to-day use. Both files are cross-verified — the trial balance at period close matches across both files dollar-for-dollar, list parity is confirmed where it should be confirmed, and any deviation between the two outputs is documented in the bundle's verification PDF rather than smoothed over silently.

Pricing on the bundle is $75.00 CAD at the launch rate (regular $149.00 CAD), which is a meaningful discount against the sum of the two standalone services ($50.00 + $50.00 = $100.00 at launch, $99.00 + $99.00 = $198.00 at regular). The discount reflects the shared diagnostic and verification overhead of running both services in a single round trip, not a quality compromise — the two deliverables are the same files you would receive if you bought the services separately, simply ordered in the correct sequence and verified together. GST or HST is added at checkout.

Turnaround is same-business-day during posted hours, which is the same target as the standalone Audit Trail Removal service. The bundle does not run the period copy and the removal in series end-to-end; the diagnostic phases overlap, and the verification gates run against both deliverables in parallel, so the wall-clock time you see in the Portal does not double when you order the bundle instead of the standalone removal. If your engagement needs a tighter clock than same-day, the Rush Delivery add-on attaches to the bundle the same way it does to any other order in this catalog.

Use this bundle whenever a CRA audit window is open or anticipated and you also want to take the opportunity to remove the audit trail from the operating file. It is also the right shape when a business sale or partner buy-in is in progress and the buyer's accountant wants a frozen period file as part of due diligence — the period copy serves that need cleanly while the post-removal main file becomes the smaller, faster operating file your practice continues using day-to-day. The pre-purchase chat in the Portal will scope the right boundary dates if your engagement is unusual.

featureSections.heading: Why the period copy runs first in the sequence
featureSections.body: Audit Trail Removal is irreversible — once the meta-history table is dropped, the per-edit history is gone for good. Producing the CRA Period Copy first, against the still-intact source file, captures the historical audit-trail state cleanly inside the period boundary so the period copy carries that evidence forward. Running the order in the opposite sequence would mean the period copy reflects the post-removal state, which is materially less useful for an actual CRA audit response. The bundle locks the sequence in so order-time mistakes that would otherwise be irrecoverable become impossible to make.

featureSections.heading: What the cross-verification step adds
featureSections.body: Each standalone service has its own verification PDF documenting the trial-balance match against the source. The bundle adds a cross-verification step that confirms the period copy's end-of-period trial balance matches the post-removal file's opening trial balance at the same date, dollar-for-dollar, on every account. That match is the structural integrity gate of the bundle: if it ever failed, neither file would leave our environment, because a mismatch would mean one of the two services produced a deviation. The cross-verification PDF documents the match line by line for direct reviewer handoff.

featureSections.heading: Pricing math against the standalone services
featureSections.body: Standalone Audit Trail Removal is $50.00 CAD at the launch rate ($99.00 CAD regular). Standalone CRA Period Copy is $50.00 CAD at the launch rate ($99.00 CAD regular). Bundle launch is $75.00 CAD against a regular price of $149.00 CAD, which is a meaningful discount over either combination of standalones. The discount reflects shared diagnostic and verification overhead, not a quality compromise — the deliverables are identical to the two standalones with the additional cross-verification step on top, ordered in the correct sequence to preserve the historical audit-trail evidence inside the period boundary.

faqs.question: Can I buy this bundle without an open CRA audit?
faqs.answer: Yes — most practices order the bundle proactively rather than reactively, often during a year-end close or a fiscal-year transition when both deliverables make sense at the same moment. The period copy serves as a frozen historical reference even outside an active audit, and the post-removal main file becomes the smaller, faster operating file your practice continues using day-to-day. Pre-purchase chat in the Portal will scope the right period boundary dates if your engagement is proactive rather than triggered by a specific CRA letter.

faqs.question: Does the bundle support custom period boundaries beyond fiscal years?
faqs.answer: Yes. The period boundary is defined by a start date and an end date plus an optional posting-cutoff timestamp, and there is no constraint that the boundary align to a fiscal year. Common non-fiscal boundaries include a partner-buy-in event date, a sale-of-business closing date, a multi-month CRA audit window that spans two fiscal years, and a retroactive period requested by an external accountant. The verification PDF echoes the boundary back in ISO format unambiguously so the deliverable matches the request exactly.

faqs.question: Is the cross-verification PDF separate from the per-service verifications?
faqs.answer: The bundle delivers three documents: the period-copy verification PDF, the audit-trail-removal verification PDF, and the bundle cross-verification PDF that confirms the two outputs reconcile against each other. All three travel together in your Portal download alongside the two .qbm files. Reviewers we have worked with consistently treat the cross-verification PDF as the high-confidence document of the engagement because it answers the integrity question that the two standalone PDFs cannot answer on their own — that the two deliverables are consistent.

faqs.question: Can I attach Rush Delivery to the bundle?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Rush Delivery add-on attaches to the bundle the same way it does to any other order in this Portal, moving the bundle to the head of the standard processing queue ahead of every non-rush ticket. The cross-verification step is still run in full — Rush only changes scheduling, not the verification work or the deliverable shape — so the rush bundle ships with all three verification documents on the faster clock. If your timing is tighter than rush would deliver, pre-purchase chat can scope a custom escalation directly.

services.name: AccountEdge/MYOB → QuickBooks
services.tagline: Move accounts, contacts, items, and history from AccountEdge or MYOB into QuickBooks Desktop.
services.description: Migrate your AccountEdge or MYOB data to QuickBooks Desktop. Transfers chart of accounts, customers, vendors, items, and transaction history.
services.deliverable: AccountEdge/MYOB data migrated into a working QuickBooks Desktop file
longDescription: AccountEdge and MYOB users in Canada have a growing problem: vendor support has narrowed, the Canadian payroll integration is no longer first-class, and the platform's gravity has shifted toward QuickBooks Desktop and Online for any practice that needs a current, well-maintained accounting platform with active third-party integrations. The AccountEdge/MYOB to QuickBooks Desktop migration in this catalog is the structured rebuild that gets your live data — chart of accounts, customers, vendors, items, employees, transaction history — out of the legacy file and into a working Premier or Pro file ready for day-one operations.

The migration accepts the source as a .zip export from AccountEdge, a .myo file from older MYOB releases, or a .myox file from current MYOB releases. Every accepted format is parsed against the same internal canonical representation, so the destination QuickBooks file is structurally identical regardless of which source format you started from. That matters when a practice has files from multiple AccountEdge or MYOB releases — the resulting QuickBooks files line up cleanly against each other and reporting at the practice level becomes consistent without a separate normalization pass.

What moves: the entire chart of accounts, every customer with addresses and balances, every vendor with terms and balances, every item with cost and price information, every employee with year-to-date payroll figures as of the migration cutoff, every posted transaction in detail through the migration boundary, sales tax codes, payment methods, and class structures where the destination edition supports them. What stays out: AccountEdge-specific or MYOB-specific UI customizations that have no QuickBooks counterpart, custom report definitions written against the source platform's report writer, and any third-party integration tied to the source platform's APIs.

Pricing is $125.00 CAD at the launch rate (regular $249.00 CAD), GST or HST added at checkout, and the turnaround target is one to two business days end-to-end. The work runs in our isolated Canadian processing environment and the deliverable is a working QuickBooks Desktop .qbm plus a written audit document that maps every non-trivial transformation back to the source — same forensic shape as the audit document that ships with the Complex Enterprise conversion and the Multi-Currency Removal service. We retain your source upload for seven days post-delivery and then permanently delete it.

The thirty-minute review call that ships with this service is where most practices land their AccountEdge or MYOB-specific questions. The call covers the per-decision audit document on your file, the verification numbers from our standard checklist, and any follow-up points your reviewing accountant raises in the days after delivery. Most engagements only need one call. Inside the fourteen-day support window, additional questions are handled through the Portal at no extra charge — we do not run a per-minute meter on platform migrations any more than we do on Complex Enterprise conversions, and the support posture is intentionally identical.

featureSections.heading: Source formats accepted and why
featureSections.body: We accept three source formats: a .zip export from AccountEdge, a .myo file from older MYOB releases (typically pre-2020), and a .myox file from current MYOB releases. Each format is parsed against the same internal canonical representation and the resulting QuickBooks Desktop file is structurally identical regardless of which source format you started from. That equivalence matters when a practice has files from multiple eras — the resulting QuickBooks files line up cleanly against each other and reporting at the practice level becomes consistent without a separate normalization pass after the migration is done.

featureSections.heading: What the audit document records
featureSections.body: The audit document maps every non-trivial transformation between the source platform and the QuickBooks destination, organized by file area: chart of accounts mapping, customer and vendor list reconciliation, item structure conversion, payroll year-to-date carryforward, sales-tax code translation, class and location handling, custom-field disposition, and any AccountEdge or MYOB feature with no QuickBooks counterpart. The document is structured so a reviewing accountant can read it linearly and walk away with a complete picture of every decision the migration made. Reviewers we have worked with consistently treat the document as sufficient evidence.

featureSections.heading: Why one to two business days, not same day
featureSections.body: Platform migrations are categorically more involved than even the Complex Enterprise conversion in this Portal because the source and destination data models are not just different versions of the same product — they are different products with different conventions for accounts, items, payroll, and sales tax. The diagnostic phase is longer, the canonical-representation pass is real engineering work, and the verification step has to confirm correctness across two structurally different schemas. One to two business days is an honest target; we do not compress it artificially to look better next to single-day services that target a much narrower scope.

faqs.question: Will my historical reports still match after the migration?
faqs.answer: Period-level historical reports — monthly P&Ls, quarterly trial balances, annual balance sheets — match the source platform's reports dollar-for-dollar at every period close as a hard verification gate. Detail-level reports against AccountEdge or MYOB-native reporting features may not have a direct QuickBooks counterpart and need to be rebuilt in QuickBooks's report writer. The audit document calls those out individually so you know which reports need a rebuild before the migration concludes. Most practices find the rebuild is straightforward once the underlying data has landed cleanly in the destination.

faqs.question: How is payroll history carried across?
faqs.answer: Every active employee comes across with year-to-date payroll figures as of the migration cutoff date, mapped onto QuickBooks Desktop's payroll item structure for Canadian payroll. Historical payroll runs prior to the cutoff are summarized rather than reposted at full per-paycheque detail, which is the standard practice for cross-platform migrations and avoids re-running source-deductions calculations against a different platform's tax engine. The audit document records the cutoff date and the summarization approach so the deliverable is auditable on payroll specifically.

faqs.question: What if my AccountEdge or MYOB file uses features QuickBooks does not have?
faqs.answer: Some AccountEdge and MYOB features have no first-party QuickBooks counterpart — particular custom report types, certain UI-level customizations, specific job-tracking shapes that overlap with QuickBooks's class and location model rather than living in their own dedicated structure. The audit document calls every one of those out, with a recommended QuickBooks-native pattern that captures the same operational intent. We do not silently invent half-mapped equivalents during processing; if a feature has no clean destination, the document names it as such and recommends the closest native pattern.

faqs.question: Can I migrate to QuickBooks Online instead of Desktop?
faqs.answer: This service targets QuickBooks Desktop (Premier or Pro) as the destination. If your end goal is QuickBooks Online, the recommended path is to migrate to Desktop first using this service, then run the QBO Readiness Report on the resulting file, then execute Intuit's Desktop-to-QBO migration with any pre-migration remediation the readiness report recommends. The two-step approach yields cleaner data than going directly cross-platform to QBO because the canonicalization step is done in a structured environment rather than during Intuit's QBO import, which has different behaviour.

services.name: Sage 50/Simply Accounting → QuickBooks
services.tagline: Lift your full Sage 50 or Simply Accounting ledger into a working QuickBooks Desktop file.
services.description: Migrate your Sage 50 (Simply Accounting) data to QuickBooks Desktop. Full transfer of chart of accounts, customers, vendors, items, and transaction history.
services.deliverable: Sage 50 / Simply Accounting data migrated into a working QuickBooks Desktop file
longDescription: Sage 50 in Canada — formerly Simply Accounting, and still called Simply by a substantial number of long-tenured Canadian bookkeepers — has been the default small-business accounting platform for a generation, and over the last few years a noticeable number of those practices have decided to consolidate onto QuickBooks Desktop instead. The reasons vary by practice (third-party integration coverage, multi-user posture on Windows, modern bank-feed connectors, the practice's own future hires), but the migration shape is consistent enough that the QuickBooks Portal carries it as a dedicated service with its own audit posture and turnaround target.

We accept three Sage 50 source formats: a .cab cabinet file (the modern Sage 50 backup format), a .zip archive (used by older Simply Accounting releases and some Sage 50 export workflows), and a .sai standalone file (occasionally produced by export tools that operate against a live Sage 50 database). All three formats are parsed against the same internal canonical representation, so the resulting QuickBooks Desktop file is structurally identical regardless of which Sage 50 era the source belongs to. That equivalence matters for practices migrating multiple files from multiple Sage 50 versions.

Sage 50's data model differs from QuickBooks's in several specific areas that our migration handles deliberately: Sage 50's account-segment structure (accounts segmented by department or division) maps to QuickBooks's class structure rather than to additional account-list entries, Sage 50's project-tracking apparatus maps to QuickBooks's job structure on the customer rather than a parallel project entity, and Sage 50's GIFI codes are preserved as a custom field on accounts rather than dropped, because Canadian practices use them for T2 corporate tax filings and they are non-trivial to recreate manually after the fact.

Pricing is $125.00 CAD at the launch rate (regular $249.00 CAD), GST or HST added at checkout, and the turnaround target is one to two business days end-to-end — same shape as the AccountEdge/MYOB migration in this Portal, because the engineering involvement is comparable. The work runs inside our isolated Canadian processing environment and the deliverable is a working QuickBooks Desktop .qbm plus a written audit document mapping every non-trivial transformation back to the Sage 50 source. We retain your source upload for seven days post-delivery and then permanently delete it.

If your Sage 50 file uses a database password or a multi-user setup with per-user permissions, please include the password and a list of active user accounts in the Portal order notes — we cannot read the file without the password, and the migration treats the active user list as the basis for the QuickBooks user setup if you ask us to recreate user accounts on the destination. The audit document records the user-mapping decision so it is auditable. Same thirty-minute review call as the AccountEdge migration, same fourteen-day support window with no per-minute meter, same money-back guarantee posture across the catalog.

featureSections.heading: Sage 50 source formats accepted
featureSections.body: We accept three Sage 50 source formats: a .cab cabinet file (the modern Sage 50 backup format produced by the platform's own backup workflow), a .zip archive (used by older Simply Accounting releases and some Sage 50 export tools), and a .sai standalone file (occasionally produced by third-party export utilities that operate directly against a live Sage 50 database). Each format is parsed against the same internal canonical representation and the resulting QuickBooks Desktop file is structurally identical, which matters when a practice has files from multiple Sage 50 versions and needs reporting consistency across the whole portfolio.

featureSections.heading: How Sage 50 specifics translate to QuickBooks
featureSections.body: Sage 50's account-segment structure maps to QuickBooks's class structure rather than to additional account-list entries, which is the right structural fit and avoids inflating the chart of accounts unnecessarily. Sage 50's project-tracking apparatus maps to QuickBooks's job structure on the customer record. GIFI codes on accounts are preserved as a QuickBooks custom field on the account because Canadian practices use them for T2 corporate tax filings and they are non-trivial to recreate manually. Each translation choice is documented in the audit document line by line so reviewing accountants see the reasoning.

featureSections.heading: Database passwords and user accounts
featureSections.body: If your Sage 50 file is password-protected, include the database password in the Portal order notes — we cannot parse a protected file without it, and the order will pause on intake otherwise. Multi-user Sage 50 setups carry a list of active users with per-user permissions; if you want us to recreate the user list on the destination QuickBooks file, include the list in the order notes too. The audit document records both decisions. Passwords supplied via Portal order notes are encrypted at rest, accessible only to the engineer running your conversion, and discarded along with the working files seven days after delivery.

faqs.question: Are GIFI codes preserved during the migration?
faqs.answer: Yes. GIFI codes on accounts are preserved as a QuickBooks custom field on the account record specifically because Canadian practices use them for T2 corporate tax filings and they are time-consuming to recreate manually after the fact. The audit document records the GIFI mapping line by line so the deliverable is auditable on tax codes specifically. If your tax software pulls GIFI codes through a custom-field lookup, the QuickBooks destination structure works with that lookup once the tax software is pointed at the new field, which is a five-minute configuration on the tax-software side.

faqs.question: Will Sage 50 project tracking work the same way in QuickBooks?
faqs.answer: Sage 50's project-tracking apparatus maps to QuickBooks's job structure on the customer record, which captures the same operational intent — tracking revenue and cost against a defined project — but uses a different navigation pattern in the QuickBooks interface. Project-level reporting is rebuilt against QuickBooks's job-based reports, which work natively without custom report definitions. The audit document calls out project mapping as a named section so reviewing accountants see the structural decision in writing rather than having to reverse-engineer it from the deliverable file later.

faqs.question: How is multi-user Sage 50 handled in the destination file?
faqs.answer: Multi-user setups in Sage 50 carry a list of active users with per-user permissions. If you supply the user list in the Portal order notes, we recreate the user accounts on the destination QuickBooks file with the closest QuickBooks-native permission set per user. If you do not supply the list, the destination file ships with a single Admin user and you set up additional users yourself inside QuickBooks afterward. Either path is valid; most practices prefer to set up users themselves so passwords are never transmitted off-site, which is also the safer default.

faqs.question: How do I produce the right Sage 50 backup before uploading?
faqs.answer: Inside Sage 50, run File → Backup, choose a folder, and let Sage 50 produce a .cab file with a date-stamped name. That .cab is the modern format and the one we recommend by default. If your release of Sage 50 produces a .zip archive instead, that is also accepted. If you need to use a third-party export tool to produce a .sai file, please confirm in the Portal order notes which tool produced the file so we know which canonical-representation parser path to apply. Pre-purchase chat in the Portal walks through the right backup workflow for your specific Sage 50 version.

services.name: 5-Pack Conversions
services.tagline: Five prepaid standard conversions for firms juggling several client files at once.
services.description: Bundle of 5 standard Enterprise → Premier/Pro conversions at a discounted rate. Perfect for accountants and bookkeepers managing multiple client files. Credits are valid for 12 months.
services.meta_description: Five standard Enterprise-to-Premier/Pro conversions at a discount. Perfect for accountants and bookkeepers. Credits valid 12 months.
longDescription: Accounting practices and bookkeeping firms tend to run their Enterprise-to-Premier conversions in clusters rather than one at a time. A single new client engagement might bring in three files at once; a year-end retainer might involve converting five client files inside a six-week window; an acquisition might require converting the acquiring firm's entire portfolio in one sprint. Buying five standalone conversions through the Portal works fine for that pattern, but the per-credit pricing shape we offer on the 5-Pack matches the practice's actual cash flow better and removes a meaningful amount of friction from the order rhythm.

Each credit in the pack redeems for one Enterprise → Premier/Pro Standard conversion, the same service id 1 you would otherwise order on its own — same engineers, same verification PDF, same two-ticket support window per redemption, same money-back guarantee. Credits are issued to your Portal account the moment the order completes (no waiting period, no separate provisioning step) and remain valid for twelve months from the purchase date. Inside that window you can redeem the credits one at a time as files arrive, or in a single batch if it suits the engagement, with no per-redemption admin overhead.

The per-credit math at the launch rate works out to $65.00 CAD per conversion on the 5-Pack ($325.00 CAD ÷ 5), against $75.00 CAD per standalone Standard conversion at the same launch promotion. The regular-price math is $129.80 CAD per credit on the 5-Pack ($649.00 CAD ÷ 5), against $149.00 CAD per standalone. The savings reflect the operational simplicity of provisioning credits in advance, not a reduction in service quality — every redeemed credit produces a full Standard conversion with the full audit document and verification gate, identical to a standalone purchase in every respect.

Volume packs are scoped to the Standard conversion only because that is the file shape practices buy in clusters. If a redemption surfaces a Complex file shape on intake (advanced inventory, multi-currency enabled, oversized lists, or non-default custom fields), the order pauses and we offer an upgrade path to the Complex tier with a transparent dollar adjustment — you do not lose the credit, you do not lose the file, and you decide whether to upgrade or to redirect that credit to a different file. Most practices appreciate the explicit pause-and-confirm, because silent auto-upgrades on a clustered engagement are a billing-surprise pattern we deliberately avoid.

The 5-Pack is the right shape for a practice with four to seven anticipated conversions inside a twelve-month window. Below four, the savings against five standalones are real but modest, and the unused-credit risk is meaningful. Above seven, the 10-Pack gives you a better per-credit rate plus the bundled Guaranteed 30-Minute turnaround on every redemption, which is enough of a structural difference to justify a separate volume tier. Pre-purchase chat in the Portal will recommend the right pack size against your forecast — we would rather sell the right pack than the most expensive one.

featureSections.heading: How credit redemption works
featureSections.body: Credits are issued to your Portal account immediately on order completion and remain valid for twelve months from the purchase date. Each credit redeems for one Standard Enterprise → Premier/Pro conversion (service id 1) — same engineers, same verification PDF, same two-ticket support window per redemption, same money-back guarantee. Redemption is a single click on a new conversion order against the credited account. Credits do not require advance scheduling and are not tied to specific client engagements at issuance, so you can route them across whichever client files arrive inside the validity window without administrative overhead.

featureSections.heading: Per-credit math at launch and regular pricing
featureSections.body: Launch-rate math: the 5-Pack at $325.00 CAD divides to $65.00 CAD per credit, against $75.00 CAD per standalone Standard conversion at the same launch promotion. Regular-rate math: the 5-Pack at $649.00 CAD divides to $129.80 CAD per credit, against $149.00 CAD per standalone. The per-credit savings reflect operational simplicity of provisioning in advance, not a reduction in service quality — every redeemed credit produces a complete Standard conversion identical to a standalone purchase in every respect, with the same audit document and verification gate written for every delivery.

featureSections.heading: Complex-file handling on a redemption
featureSections.body: Volume packs are scoped to the Standard conversion only. If a redemption uploads a file that surfaces a Complex shape on intake — advanced inventory, multi-currency, oversized lists, non-default custom fields — the order pauses and we offer an upgrade path to the Complex tier with a transparent dollar adjustment. You do not lose the credit if you decline the upgrade; the credit is returned to your account and you redirect it to a different Standard-shaped file. We never silently auto-upgrade a credit redemption to a higher tier because surprise billing on a clustered engagement is the worst experience we can provide.

faqs.question: Are 5-Pack credits transferable between client engagements?
faqs.answer: Yes. Credits live on your Portal account, not on a specific client engagement, so you can redeem them across whichever client files arrive inside the twelve-month validity window. Practices commonly route credits across multiple client files inside a single fiscal year and across multiple fiscal years if the validity window straddles two. The audit document on each redemption records the client identifier we ran against, so the practice's internal billing system can attribute credit usage to the right client engagement after the fact without ambiguity in the trail.

faqs.question: What happens to unused credits after twelve months?
faqs.answer: Unused credits expire at the end of the twelve-month validity window from the purchase date. Pre-expiry, the Portal sends reminder emails at sixty, thirty, and seven days remaining so the expiration is never a surprise. If your firm's conversion volume is lighter than the pack size implies on the buying side, the right answer is a smaller engagement or standalones rather than rolling over expired credits — the per-credit savings are sized against actual usage, and stretching the window artificially would erode the operational simplicity that makes the pack pricing sustainable in the first place.

faqs.question: Can I redeem 5-Pack credits for the Guaranteed 30-Minute service?
faqs.answer: No. The 5-Pack is scoped to the Standard Enterprise → Premier/Pro conversion only because that is the file shape practices buy in clusters. The Guaranteed 30-Minute Conversion has its own dedicated processing lane with sized engineering capacity, and bundling it into a volume pack would either misprice the lane or compromise the time guarantee. If your practice routinely needs the thirty-minute service, the 10-Pack includes the bundled Guaranteed 30-Minute turnaround on every redemption — which is the structural difference that justifies a separate volume tier.

faqs.question: Do redemptions count separately for the support and money-back guarantee?
faqs.answer: Yes. Each redeemed credit produces a complete Standard conversion that ships with its own two-ticket support window inside fourteen days and its own money-back guarantee — exactly the same posture as a standalone purchase. Redemptions do not pool into a single shared support window or a single guarantee scope, because pooling would change the support shape against the practice's actual usage patterns. The Extended Support add-on attaches to individual redemptions the same way it attaches to standalone Standard orders.

services.name: 10-Pack Conversions
services.tagline: Ten prepaid standard conversions billed at our lowest per-file rate for accounting firms.
services.description: Bundle of 10 standard Enterprise → Premier/Pro conversions at the best value. Ideal for accounting firms with a steady volume of client conversions. Credits are valid for 12 months.
services.meta_description: Ten standard Enterprise-to-Premier/Pro conversions at the best value for accounting firms. Credits valid 12 months.
longDescription: Accounting firms with a steady book of Enterprise-using clients run conversions as a year-round workflow rather than a clustered project. Ten files in a calendar year is a realistic floor for a mid-sized Canadian practice with even a modest QuickBooks-Desktop-using client base, especially during the years following a Premier or Pro release that tightens compatibility against older Enterprise file formats. The 10-Pack Conversions bundle is sized for that ongoing rhythm, with the lowest per-credit rate in the catalog and a structural feature that the 5-Pack does not include: the Guaranteed 30-Minute Conversion turnaround on every redemption.

Each credit in the 10-Pack redeems for one Enterprise → Premier/Pro Standard conversion run on the dedicated thirty-minute processing lane, not the standard queue. That difference is more than a scheduling preference — it is a structural change in how the redemptions land. Same engineers, same verification PDF, same two-ticket support window, same money-back guarantee, but every redemption ships within thirty minutes of upload completion during posted business hours. The thirty-minute promise is the same one written into the standalone Guaranteed 30-Minute Conversion service in this catalog, on every credit you redeem from the pack.

The per-credit math at the launch rate works out to $60.00 CAD per conversion on the 10-Pack ($600.00 CAD ÷ 10), against $65.00 CAD per credit on the 5-Pack and $125.00 CAD for a standalone Guaranteed 30-Minute order at the launch promotion. The regular-price math is $119.90 CAD per credit on the 10-Pack ($1,199.00 CAD ÷ 10), against $129.80 CAD per credit on the 5-Pack. The savings reflect both the operational simplicity of provisioning credits in advance and the predictability of running a steady-state thirty-minute lane against a known forward-volume commitment, which is materially easier to staff than spot demand.

Volume-pack credits are scoped to the Standard conversion only, exactly as the 5-Pack is. If a redemption surfaces a Complex file shape on intake (advanced inventory, multi-currency enabled, oversized lists, non-default custom fields), the order pauses and we offer an explicit upgrade path with a transparent dollar adjustment. You do not lose the credit if you decline; we return it to your Portal account and you redirect to a different file. Silent auto-upgrades on a clustered engagement remain something we deliberately avoid, because surprise billing inside a steady-state retainer is the worst experience we can provide.

The 10-Pack is the right shape for a practice with eight or more anticipated conversions inside a twelve-month window, especially when at least some of those conversions sit on a meaningful external deadline. The bundled thirty-minute lane converts a per-pack saving against the 5-Pack into a per-engagement velocity advantage that pays for itself the first time a partner-review meeting hangs on getting a file ready in the next forty-five minutes. Below eight conversions, the 5-Pack is a better fit; above twelve or so, pre-purchase chat in the Portal scopes a custom retainer arrangement directly.

featureSections.heading: What the bundled thirty-minute lane changes
featureSections.body: Every credit in the 10-Pack redeems on the dedicated Guaranteed 30-Minute processing lane rather than the standard queue. Same engineers, same verification PDF, same two-ticket support window per redemption, same money-back guarantee — every redemption simply ships within thirty minutes of upload completion during posted business hours. The thirty-minute promise on each credit is the same written guarantee that ships with the standalone Guaranteed 30-Minute Conversion service. If we miss it on a credit redemption for a reason on our side, that credit is refunded as a separate line item and the converted file remains yours.

featureSections.heading: Per-credit math at launch and regular pricing
featureSections.body: Launch-rate math: the 10-Pack at $600.00 CAD divides to $60.00 CAD per credit, against $65.00 CAD per credit on the 5-Pack and $125.00 CAD for a standalone Guaranteed 30-Minute Conversion at the same launch promotion. Regular-rate math: the 10-Pack at $1,199.00 CAD divides to $119.90 CAD per credit, against $129.80 CAD per credit on the 5-Pack. The savings reflect the predictability of running a steady-state thirty-minute lane against a known forward-volume commitment, which is materially easier to capacity-plan than spot demand at the same posture.

featureSections.heading: When the 10-Pack is the wrong call
featureSections.body: Skip the 10-Pack if your forecast volume is below eight conversions inside the twelve-month validity window — at that volume, the 5-Pack is a better fit even before accounting for the unused-credit risk on a larger pack. Skip it also if your conversion volume is structurally above twelve per year on an ongoing basis, because that is a retainer shape rather than a volume-pack shape, and the right posture is a custom arrangement scoped through pre-purchase chat in the Portal. Honest sizing is what keeps the bundled thirty-minute lane sustainable for everyone using the pack at the right scale.

faqs.question: Does every 10-Pack credit really get the thirty-minute guarantee?
faqs.answer: Yes — every credit in the 10-Pack redeems on the dedicated Guaranteed 30-Minute processing lane rather than the standard queue, with the same written guarantee that ships with the standalone Guaranteed 30-Minute Conversion service. The guarantee is on each individual credit, not on the pack as a whole, so missing the target on one redemption refunds that credit specifically without affecting the other nine. The structural difference between the 5-Pack and the 10-Pack is exactly this lane assignment, and it is the reason the 10-Pack is sized for steady-state firm usage.

faqs.question: How does the 10-Pack compare to a custom retainer arrangement?
faqs.answer: The 10-Pack is the right tier for a forecast of eight to twelve conversions inside a twelve-month window. Above twelve per year on an ongoing basis, a custom retainer arrangement scoped through pre-purchase chat in the Portal is materially better — capacity is reserved, billing is monthly rather than upfront, and the engineer rotation can be assigned to your firm specifically for the retainer's duration. We will recommend the retainer over a 10-Pack during pre-purchase chat if your forecast supports it, because matching tier to forecast is what keeps the per-credit pricing honest at every scale.

faqs.question: Can 10-Pack credits be downgraded to standard-queue redemptions for cost savings?
faqs.answer: No. The 10-Pack is structurally a thirty-minute-lane bundle, and credit redemption always runs on that lane — there is no in-Portal toggle to drop a redemption to the standard queue in exchange for a different cost. The lane assignment is what makes the per-credit pricing sustainable on the supply side. If your engagement does not need the thirty-minute lane on most files, the right tier is the 5-Pack at the standard-queue rate; the 10-Pack should be sized against actual thirty-minute-lane usage, not against trying to extract a discount on standard-queue work.

faqs.question: What happens if a redemption misses the thirty-minute target?
faqs.answer: The miss-the-target rules on a credit redemption are the same as on a standalone Guaranteed 30-Minute Conversion order. If the converted file is not in your Portal download area within thirty minutes of upload completion during posted business hours, for any reason on our side, the credit specifically is refunded automatically as a separate line item and the converted file remains yours. The miss does not affect the other nine credits in the pack, does not extend any other credit's validity window, and does not require you to file a separate ticket — the SLA is auditable from timestamps.
